content: Let me start by reading the necessary files to understand my instructions and the project context.
I'll read the instruction files and then examine the Lean file and blueprint chapter to produce the comparison report.

1	---
2	name: lean-vs-blueprint-checker
3	description: Per-file bidirectional verifier. Reads one .lean file and its blueprint chapter; reports (a) whether the Lean file follows the blueprint — fake/placeholder statements, signature mismatches with \lean{...} hints, proof divergence, "temporary" defs — AND (b) whether the blueprint chapter is detailed enough to have guided a faithful formalization of this file.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended in the review phase. Dispatch one checker
11	    per `.lean` file that received prover work this iteration. Each
12	    dispatch is independent and parallel-able.
13	
14	    **You may skip me this iter when NO `.lean` file received prover
15	    work** (e.g. the prover phase was skipped, or every prover
16	    INCOMPLETE'd without committing edits). Record under `## Subagent
17	    skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`:
18	    ``- lean-vs-blueprint-checker: no .lean files modified this iter
19	    (no prover edits to verify)``.
20	
21	    Do NOT skip a per-file dispatch when the prover DID commit edits
22	    to that file — skipping a prover-touched file is the review-phase
23	    failure this affordance exists to surface, not to enable. The
24	    "highly recommended" framing applies to the whole-iter decision,
25	    not to picking-and-choosing within an iter that has multiple
26	    prover-touched files.
27	  - Also dispatch on any file a code-audit subagent in your catalog
28	    flagged as suspicious, even if it received no prover work this
29	    iter.
30	  - The directive must name exactly one Lean file and its blueprint
31	    chapter — no global context, no strategy snapshot. My value is
32	    the narrow file-vs-chapter view.
33	  - I report bidirectionally: Lean → blueprint AND blueprint → Lean.
34	    The blueprint can be the failure (too thin to guide
35	    formalization, missing the level of detail the Lean code clearly
36	    needed), not only the Lean. If I flag a chapter as inadequate,
37	    the plan agent should dispatch the catalog's blueprint-writing
38	    subagent to address it.
39	  - Any must-fix-this-iter finding I report blocks downstream work on
40	    the affected file/chapter until addressed. Soft enforcement: the
41	    plan agent reads my report and follows the gate rules in plan.md.
42	---
43	
44	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
45	
46	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
47	
48	## Your Job
49	
50	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
51	
52	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
53	
54	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
55	
56	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
57	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
58	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
59	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
60	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
61	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
62	
63	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
64	
65	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
66	
67	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
68	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
69	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
70	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
71	
72	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
73	
74	## Directive Format
75	
76	```markdown
77	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker Directive
78	
79	## Slug
80	<slug>
81	
82	## Lean file
83	<path/to/file.lean>
84	
85	## Blueprint chapter
86	blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex
87	
88	## Known issues
89	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
90	```
91	
92	That's it. No strategy snapshot, no references, no full project context — just the two files and any pre-known issues to skip. Cross-file consistency is the job of the code-audit and blueprint-review subagents in the catalog; you stay focused on this one pair.
93	
94	## What you do
95	
96	1. Read your directive.
97	2. Read the Lean file completely. Note every declaration (`def`, `theorem`, `lemma`, `instance`, `axiom`, …) and its signature.
98	3. Read the blueprint chapter completely. List every `\lean{...}` reference and its corresponding declaration block.
99	4. **For each `\lean{...}` block**: locate the Lean declaration. Verify:
100	   - It exists.
101	   - Its signature matches the informal statement (use `archon-lean-lsp` for hover/signature info).
102	   - If the Lean has a body (proof or definition body), does it match the blueprint's prose? Naming and tactic choice can differ; the *mathematical content* should match.
103	5. **For each declaration in the Lean file**: is it `\lean{...}`-referenced from the blueprint? Unreferenced declarations are notable but not necessarily wrong (helpers may exist). Flag suspect ones.
104	6. **Scan for red flags** in the Lean file:
105	   - `:= sorry` where the blueprint claims a substantive statement (placeholder).
106	   - Suspect bodies like `:= True`, `:= rfl` for non-trivial claims, `:= Classical.choice _`.
107	   - Comments excusing wrong/incomplete code: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- will fix later`, `-- this is wrong but works for now`.
108	   - `axiom` declarations.
109	7. **Cross-check** any `import` paths and namespace usage that should match what the blueprint's `\lean{...}` hints suggest.
110	
111	You may use:
112	- `archon-lean-lsp` MCP tools (read-only): hover, signature lookup, diagnostics.
113	- `sorry_analyzer` if available, to count sorries in the file.
114	
115	You may NOT modify the Lean file, the blueprint, or any other project file. Even when a fix is obvious.
116	
117	## Report format
118	
119	Write your report to `.archon/task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-<slug>.md` (or the parent-aware path when invoked nested — your invocation prompt names the exact path).
120	
121	```markdown
122	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Check Report
123	
124	## Slug
125	<slug>
126	
127	## Iteration
128	<NNN>
129	
130	## Files audited
131	- Lean: <path>
132	- Blueprint: <path>
133	
134	## Per-declaration
135	
136	For every `\lean{...}` block in the chapter, one entry:
137	
138	### `\lean{Foo.bar}` (chapter: \thm:bar)
139	- **Lean target exists**: yes / no (if no: declaration name in the .lean that comes closest, or "none found")
140	- **Signature matches**: yes / partial / no (with one line on the mismatch)
141	- **Proof follows sketch**: yes / partial / no / N/A (if N/A: chapter doesn't have a proof body to compare against)
142	- **notes**: <free-form, one line per observation>
143	
144	### `\lean{Foo.baz}` ...
145	
146	## Red flags
147	
148	(Section appears only if there are findings.)
149	
150	### Placeholder / suspect bodies
151	- `Foo.bar` at line 42: body is `:= sorry` but blueprint claims a substantive proof.
152	- `Foo.qux` at line 78: defined as `:= True`, suspect.
153	- ...
154	
155	### Excuse-comments
156	- `Foo.lean:113`: comment "TODO: replace with real def" attached to `Foo.frob`. Blueprint claims `Foo.frob` is the actual definition, not a placeholder.
157	- ...
158	
159	### Axioms / Classical.choice on non-trivial claims
160	- `Foo.lean:200`: `axiom` declaration `Foo.skip_step` introduced. Blueprint does not authorize this.
161	
162	## Unreferenced declarations (informational)
163	
164	<list of declarations in the Lean file that no `\lean{...}` references. Most are helpers — only flag the ones whose name suggests they should be in the blueprint.>
165	
166	## Blueprint adequacy for this file
167	
168	A bidirectional check: does the blueprint chapter give a prover enough detail to formalize this file correctly?
169	
170	- **Coverage**: <N>/<M> Lean declarations have a corresponding `\lean{...}` block in the chapter. Unreferenced declarations: <count> helpers (acceptable) + <count> substantive (flagged below).
171	- **Proof-sketch depth**: <one of: adequate / under-specified / silent>. If under-specified or silent, list which `\thm:...` blocks need expansion.
172	- **Hint precision**: <one of: precise / loose / wrong>. Loose hints (the prose pins one Mathlib predicate but the `\lean{...}` doesn't, leaving the prover to guess) get flagged. Wrong hints (the `\lean{...}` names a declaration whose signature doesn't match the prose) are critical.
173	- **Generality**: <one of: matches need / too narrow / too broad>. If too narrow, name the parallel API that the project ended up writing because the blueprint didn't cover the needed level of generality.
174	- **Recommended chapter-side actions**: <bullet list of items a blueprint-writing subagent should land if you flagged anything above>
175	
176	## Severity summary
177	
178	Apply these rules verbatim. Do NOT under-classify to soften the blow — the plan agent's gates depend on accurate severity, and a hidden critical finding hardens into shipped wrong code.
179	
180	- **must-fix-this-iter** — every one of the following lands here, no exceptions:
181	  - Placeholder body (`:= sorry`, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim, suspect `:= Classical.choice _` patterns) on a declaration the blueprint claims is substantive.
182	  - Signature mismatch with the blueprint's prose — including using the wrong Mathlib predicate (e.g. `Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n`) when the prose pins one specific predicate.
183	  - Excuse-comments (`-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- temporary`, `-- placeholder`, `-- wrong but works for now`) on declarations the blueprint claims are real.
184	  - Axioms or `Classical.choice _` on substantive claims the blueprint does NOT authorize.
185	  - Blueprint adequacy failure: chapter is so under-specified that a prover could not have formalized this file correctly from prose alone.
186	  - Weakened-wrong definitions: Lean defines a structurally-different stand-in (e.g. `Scheme.LineBundle := CommRing.Pic Γ(X,⊤)` when the blueprint defines it as an invertible quasi-coherent O_X-module).
187	- **major** — partial signature mismatches that are fixable in-place, missing `\lean{...}` references to declarations that the blueprint should reference, comments that are stale but not actively misleading.
188	- **minor** — naming drift, helpers worth promoting to blueprint blocks, low-impact prose-vs-Lean differences.
189	
190	Overall verdict: one sentence.
191	```
192	
193	The plan agent's per-file gate (see plan.md and the blueprint-review subagent's `dispatcher_notes` in the catalog) treats every must-fix-this-iter finding as blocking. If you classify a wrong signature as "major" to avoid the gate firing, you are working against the project, not for it.
194	
195	## Return value
196	
197	Your final assistant message:
198	
199	- One line: `<slug>: <overall verdict> — <N> declarations checked, <M> red flags`
200	- The path to your full report.
201	
202	## Reminders
203	
204	- **One file pair, no global context.** Cross-file consistency belongs to the catalog's code-audit and blueprint-review subagents.
205	- **You are read-only.** No project source, no blueprint, no state files (except your own report).
206	- **Excuse-comments are red flags, not workflow.** Treat "we use a wrong def for now" with the suspicion it deserves.
207	- **Mathematical content match, not syntactic.** The Lean proof can choose different tactics than the chapter's sketch implies, as long as the mathematical steps are the same.
208

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Under Claude Code, skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Under Claude Code, rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — under Claude Code this is installed as the `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source), providing slash commands `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc. Under other harnesses the slash-command surface is absent; the underlying skill scripts are still on disk under `.claude/skills/` and can be run directly from the shell.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts — that path is the same whatever harness you run under. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) surface as `mcp__*` tools: under Claude Code they are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json`; under other harnesses the loop wires the same servers in at launch.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned agent reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**A dispatch is a BLOCKING Bash call — wait by staying inside it.** `archon-subagent.py` is synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and returns only once the child finishes and its report is on disk). A long dispatch may be auto-backgrounded with a task ID — that's fine; wait for that task's result before acting. **Do NOT use the `Monitor` tool to wait** — it is non-blocking (returns immediately, "keep working"), so your turn ends and the subagents are abandoned mid-run. **Do NOT use foreground `sleep` / `until … ; do sleep … ; done`** — the harness blocks `sleep`. To run independent subagents **in parallel**, put all their dispatches in **one Bash call**, each backgrounded with `&`, ending with `wait` — e.g. `python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name A … & python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py --name B … & wait` — which blocks until all finish (the whole wave as one blocking dispatch). Serialize only subagents that write the **same file** (separate sequential calls). The rule never changes: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required.
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read (edits via `archon discuss`) |
57	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
58	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
60	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
61	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
62	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
65	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
66	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
67	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
68	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
69	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
70	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
71	
72	### Subagent permissions
73	
74	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
75	
76	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
77	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
78	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
79	
80	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
81	
82	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper.
83	
84	## Protected Declarations
85	
86	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
87	
88	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
89	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
90	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
91	
92	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
93	
94	Two semantic markers:
95	
96	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
97	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
98	
99	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
100	
101	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake build <module>` (it uses `lake build`, not `lake env lean <file>`, so dependencies are built and missing-import failures aren't spurious), and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents should NOT add or remove `\leanok` as routine bookkeeping — leave it to the sync. The one exception is the review agent: if it is **certain** the sync got a marker wrong (a clearly-finished proof left unmarked, or a marker present on something incomplete), it may manually correct it, but must explicitly surface and justify that override in its summary (see `.archon/prompts/review.md`).
102	
103	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
104	
105	## User Interaction
106	
107	The user provides hints in two places:
108	
109	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
110	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
111	
112	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
113	
114	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
115	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true or important at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
116	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
117	
118	## Agent Roles
119	
120	### Plan Agent
121	
122	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
123	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
124	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
125	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
126	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
127	- Curate `ARCHON_MEMORY.md` — the condensed, cross-iteration knowledge file injected (read-only) into every agent's prompt. Keep only durable facts that would surprise an agent reading the code fresh (dead-end tactics, files not to touch, Mathlib gap coordinates, protected invariants). Hard limits: ~10 bullets / ~600 chars; prune before adding. Do NOT duplicate what's obvious from the code or PROGRESS.md.
128	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
129	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
130	
131	### Prover Agent
132	
133	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
134	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
135	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
136	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
137	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
138	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
139	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
140	
141	### Subagents
142	
143	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
144	
145	### Review Agent
146	
147	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
148	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
149	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
150	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
151	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Normally leave `\leanok` to the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase; only override a marker the sync got demonstrably wrong, and justify it in your summary.
152	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
153	
154	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
155	
156	These run automatically between phases:
157	
158	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
159	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
160	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

5	/-!
6	# The tautological quotient and the universal property of `Gr(r,d)`
7	
8	This file adds, on top of the Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)` built in
9	`GrassmannianCells.lean`, the tautological rank-`d` quotient
10	`u : O^r ↠ U` and the universal property making `Gr(d,r)` the fine moduli
11	space of rank-`d` locally free quotients of `O^r`.
12	
13	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianQuot.tex` (Nitsure §1).
14	-/
15	
16	universe u
17	
18	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
19	
20	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
21	
22	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — scalar endomorphisms of the structure sheaf
23	
24	To realise a matrix of regular functions as a morphism of free sheaves of modules we
25	need to turn a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` of the structure sheaf into a scalar
26	endomorphism of `O_X` (= `SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf`). On the affine chart
27	`U^I = Spec R^I` the matrix entries of the universal matrix `X^I` live in `R^I`, and we
28	inject them into the global sections via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`.
29	
30	These helpers are project-local: Mathlib has no ready-made "matrix ↦ morphism of free
31	sheaves" primitive. -/
32	
33	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
34	
35	/-- The global section of the structure sheaf `O_X` (as a sheaf of modules over itself)
36	determined by a section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)` over the whole space, by restriction to every open.
37	Project-local helper for building scalar endomorphisms. -/
38	noncomputable def globalUnitSection (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
39	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).sections :=
40	  PresheafOfModules.sectionsMk
41	    (fun Y => (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
42	    (by
43	      intro Y Z f
44	      change (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map f) (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
45	        = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a
46	      rw [← CategoryTheory.comp_apply, ← X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map_comp]
47	      congr 1)
48	
49	/-- The scalar endomorphism of `O_X` given by a global section `a ∈ Γ(X, ⊤)`:
50	multiplication by `a`. Project-local helper. -/
51	noncomputable def scalarEnd (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
52	    SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
53	  (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.symm (globalUnitSection a)
54	
55	/-- `scalarEnd 1` is the identity endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for
56	identifying the diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
57	lemma scalarEnd_one : scalarEnd (1 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) := by
58	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
59	  ext Y
60	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (1 : Γ(X, ⊤))
61	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val (𝟙 (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf))).app Y
62	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
63	  rw [SheafOfModules.id_val, PresheafOfModules.id_app]
64	  exact map_one _
65	
66	/-- `scalarEnd 0` is the zero endomorphism of `O_X`. Project-local helper for identifying
67	the off-diagonal entries of the chart quotient. -/
68	lemma scalarEnd_zero : scalarEnd (0 : Γ(X, ⊤)) = 0 := by
69	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
70	  ext Y
71	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (0 : Γ(X, ⊤))
72	      = (SheafOfModules.Hom.val
73	          (0 : SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)).app Y
74	          (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
75	  refine (map_zero _).trans ?_
76	  rfl
77	
78	/-- The value of the scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` on a section `x` over `Y` is
79	multiplication by the restriction of `a`: `(scalarEnd a)(x) = x · a|_Y`. Project-local
80	helper, the computational heart of the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities below. -/
81	lemma scalarEnd_val_app (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ)
82	    (x : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y) :
83	    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y x = x * X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
84	  rfl
85	
86	/-- `scalarEnd c` corresponds to the global section `c` under `unitHomEquiv`. -/
87	lemma unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd (c : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
88	    (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd c) = globalUnitSection c := by
89	  rw [scalarEnd, Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
90	
91	/-- The scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` sends the unit section `1` to `a|_Y`. -/
92	lemma scalarEnd_val_app_one (a : Γ(X, ⊤)) (Y : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (X : TopCat))ᵒᵖ) :
93	    (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
94	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a := by
95	  exact one_smul _ _
96	
97	/-- Composition of scalar endomorphisms is multiplication: `scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b =
98	scalarEnd (a * b)`. Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` multiplicativity. -/
99	lemma scalarEnd_comp (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
100	    scalarEnd a ≫ scalarEnd b = scalarEnd (a * b) := by
101	  apply (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv.injective
102	  change SheafOfModules.sectionsMap (scalarEnd b)
103	        ((SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd a))
104	      = (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf).unitHomEquiv (scalarEnd (a * b))
105	  rw [unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd, unitHomEquiv_scalarEnd]
106	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
107	  change (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op a)
108	      = X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a * b)
109	  exact (RingHom.map_mul (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b).symm
110	
111	/-- `scalarEnd` is additive: `scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b`.
112	Project-local; underlies `matrixEnd` matrix-multiplication identity. -/
113	lemma scalarEnd_add (a b : Γ(X, ⊤)) :
114	    scalarEnd (a + b) = scalarEnd a + scalarEnd b := by
115	  conv_lhs => rw [scalarEnd]
116	  rw [Equiv.symm_apply_eq]
117	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
118	  change X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (a + b)
119	      = (scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
120	        + (scalarEnd b).val.app Y (1 : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y)
121	  rw [scalarEnd_val_app_one, scalarEnd_val_app_one]
122	  exact RingHom.map_add (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op).hom a b
123	
124	/-- `scalarEnd` of a finite sum is the sum of the `scalarEnd`s. Project-local. -/
125	lemma scalarEnd_sum {ι : Type*} (s : Finset ι) (f : ι → Γ(X, ⊤)) :
126	    scalarEnd (∑ i ∈ s, f i) = ∑ i ∈ s, scalarEnd (f i) := by
127	  classical
128	  induction s using Finset.induction with
129	  | empty => simp [scalarEnd_zero]
130	  | insert a s ha ih => rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, scalarEnd_add, ih]
131	
132	/-! ## Matrix automorphisms of the free sheaf
133	
134	To realise the `GL_d` bundle transitions we promote an (invertible) `d × d` matrix of
135	global sections to an automorphism of the free rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`, exactly as
136	`chartQuotientMap` realises the universal matrix. The two key algebraic facts — that
137	`matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition and the identity matrix into the
138	identity — follow from the `scalarEnd` ring-hom identities above. -/
139	
140	/-- `SheafOfModules` over `O_S` has finite biproducts (it has finite products). -/
141	instance hasFiniteBiproducts_modules (S : Scheme.{0}) :
142	    HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
143	  HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
144	
145	/-- A `d × d` matrix of global sections of `O_S` realised as an endomorphism of the free
146	rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`: the `(p,q)`-entry acts as `scalarEnd`, assembled over the rank-`d`
147	biproduct (mirrors `chartQuotientMap`). Project-local. -/
148	noncomputable def matrixEnd {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
149	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶
150	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
151	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
152	    biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i)) ≫
153	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom
154	
155	/-- Composition of two `biproduct.matrix` morphisms is the matrix of pointwise sums of
156	composites — the categorical matrix product. Project-local helper for `matrixEnd_comp`. -/
157	private lemma biproduct_matrix_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
158	    (mM mN : Fin d → Fin d →
159	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)) :
160	    biproduct.matrix (f := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)
161	        (g := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) mM ≫ biproduct.matrix mN
162	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i q => ∑ p, mM i p ≫ mN p q) := by
163	  refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun q => ?_))
164	  simp only [Category.assoc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.matrix_π, biproduct.lift_desc,
165	    biproduct.ι_matrix, biproduct.lift_π]
166	
167	/-- `matrixEnd` turns matrix multiplication into composition (with the order reversed by
168	the contravariance of the column/component indexing): `matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N =
169	matrixEnd (N * M)`. Project-local. -/
170	lemma matrixEnd_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
171	    matrixEnd M ≫ matrixEnd N = matrixEnd (N * M) := by
172	  rw [matrixEnd, matrixEnd, matrixEnd]
173	  have hcomp : biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))
174	        ≫ biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (N p i))
175	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd ((N * M) p i)) := by
176	    rw [biproduct_matrix_comp]
177	    congr 1
178	    funext i q
179	    simp_rw [scalarEnd_comp]
180	    rw [← scalarEnd_sum, Matrix.mul_apply]
181	    exact congrArg scalarEnd (Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun p _ => mul_comm _ _))
182	  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
183	  rw [← Category.assoc (biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i))), hcomp]
184	
185	/-- `matrixEnd` of the identity matrix is the identity. Project-local. -/
186	lemma matrixEnd_one {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} :
187	    matrixEnd (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) = 𝟙 _ := by
188	  rw [matrixEnd]
189	  have hmat : biproduct.matrix
190	        (fun i p => scalarEnd ((1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) p i))
191	      = 𝟙 (⨁ fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) := by
192	    refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_))
193	    simp only [Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.lift_π]
194	    rw [Matrix.one_apply]
195	    by_cases h : p = i
196	    · subst h; rw [if_pos rfl, scalarEnd_one, biproduct.ι_π_self]
197	    · rw [if_neg h, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm h)]
198	  rw [hmat, Category.id_comp, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.inv_hom_id]
199	
200	/-- An invertible `d × d` matrix of global sections induces an automorphism of the free
201	rank-`d` sheaf `O_S^d`. Project-local — the `GL_d` data underlying the bundle transitions. -/
202	noncomputable def matrixToFreeIso {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
203	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
204	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ≅
205	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) where
206	  hom := matrixEnd M
207	  inv := matrixEnd N
208	  hom_inv_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hNM, matrixEnd_one]
209	  inv_hom_id := by rw [matrixEnd_comp, hMN, matrixEnd_one]
210	
211	@[simp] lemma matrixToFreeIso_hom {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
212	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
213	    (matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM).hom = matrixEnd M := rfl
214	
215	/-- **Matrix automorphisms compose multiplicatively** (`lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul`): the
216	forward maps of two matrix automorphisms compose to `matrixEnd` of the matrix product (with
217	the order reversed by the column/component contravariance). This is the linkage that turns
218	the matrix-level Cramer-inverse cocycle into a composition identity of sheaf-of-module
219	isomorphisms. Project-local. -/
220	lemma matrixToFreeIso_mul {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
221	    (A A' B B' : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤))
222	    (hAA' : A * A' = 1) (hA'A : A' * A = 1) (hBB' : B * B' = 1) (hB'B : B' * B = 1) :
223	    (matrixToFreeIso A A' hAA' hA'A).hom ≫ (matrixToFreeIso B B' hBB' hB'B).hom
224	      = matrixEnd (B * A) := by
225	  rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixToFreeIso_hom, matrixEnd_comp]
226	
227	@[simp] lemma matrixToFreeIso_inv {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ}
228	    (M N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) (hMN : M * N = 1) (hNM : N * M = 1) :
229	    (matrixToFreeIso M N hMN hNM).inv = matrixEnd N := rfl
230	
231	/-! ## The rectangular matrix homomorphism of free sheaves
232	
233	The chart quotient `u^I` is "left multiplication by the `d × r` universal matrix `X^I`";
234	to manipulate it under pullback and against the square `GL_d` transitions we generalise
235	the square `matrixEnd` API to rectangular matrices (`def:gr_matrixEndRect`,
236	`lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp`; the pullback naturality `lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback` comes
237	after the scalar atom below). -/
238	
239	/-- A `d × r` matrix of global sections of `O_S` realised as a morphism of free sheaves
240	`O_S^r ⟶ O_S^d` (`def:gr_matrixEndRect`): the `(p,q)`-entry acts as `scalarEnd`, assembled
241	over the two biproducts exactly as the square `matrixEnd` and the chart quotient
242	`chartQuotientMap` (which is by construction `matrixEndRect` of the injected universal
243	matrix). Project-local. -/
244	noncomputable def matrixEndRect {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
245	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
246	    SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
247	      SheafOfModules.free (R := S.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
248	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
249	    biproduct.matrix (fun i p => scalarEnd (M p i)) ≫
250	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom
251	
252	/-- Composition of two `biproduct.matrix` morphisms with a rectangular first factor —
253	the categorical matrix product. Project-local helper for `matrixEndRect_comp`. -/
254	private lemma biproduct_matrix_comp_rect {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
255	    (mM : Fin r → Fin d →
256	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf))
257	    (mN : Fin d → Fin d →
258	      (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)) :
259	    biproduct.matrix (f := fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)
260	        (g := fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) mM ≫ biproduct.matrix mN
261	      = biproduct.matrix (fun i q => ∑ p, mM i p ≫ mN p q) := by
262	  refine biproduct.hom_ext' _ _ (fun i => biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun q => ?_))
263	  simp only [Category.assoc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.matrix_π, biproduct.lift_desc,
264	    biproduct.ι_matrix, biproduct.lift_π]
265	
266	/-- **Square-after-rectangular composition law** (`lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp`):
267	`matrixEndRect M ≫ matrixEnd N = matrixEndRect (N * M)` for `M : d × r`, `N : d × d` —
268	the matrix product, with the order reversed by the contravariance of the column/component
269	indexing exactly as in `matrixEnd_comp`. Project-local. -/
270	@[reassoc]
271	lemma matrixEndRect_comp {S : Scheme.{0}} {d r : ℕ}
272	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) Γ(S, ⊤)) (N : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤)) :
273	    matrixEndRect M ≫ matrixEnd N = matrixEndRect (N * M) := by
274	  rw [matrixEndRect, matrixEnd, matrixEndRect]
275	  have hcomp : biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin r) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd (M p i))
276	        ≫ biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin d) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd (N p i))
277	      = biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin r) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd ((N * M) p i)) := by
278	    rw [biproduct_matrix_comp_rect]
279	    congr 1
280	    funext i q
281	    simp_rw [scalarEnd_comp]
282	    rw [← scalarEnd_sum, Matrix.mul_apply]
283	    exact congrArg scalarEnd (Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun p _ => mul_comm _ _))
284	  simp only [Category.assoc, Iso.symm_hom, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
285	  rw [← Category.assoc (biproduct.matrix (fun (i : Fin r) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd (M p i))),
286	    hcomp]
287	
288	/-! ## The tautological quotient on the charts -/
289	
290	/-- The **chart quotient** `u^I : O_{U^I}^r → O_{U^I}^d` (`def:gr_chart_quotient`):
291	left multiplication by the universal matrix `X^I` (`universalMatrix`). It is realised as
292	the morphism of free sheaves of modules whose matrix of components, in the standard bases
293	`(e_{i'})_{i' : Fin r}` and `(e_p)_{p : Fin d}`, is the universal matrix `X^I` injected
294	into the structure sheaf via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. Since the `I`-minor of `X^I` is the
295	identity, `u^I` is a split surjection onto the free rank-`d` sheaf.
296	
297	Project-local: Mathlib has no "matrix ↦ morphism of free sheaves" primitive. -/
298	noncomputable def chartQuotientMap (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
299	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶
300	      SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
301	  let A := CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
302	  let R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf
303	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules R) :=
304	    HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
305	  let M : ∀ (_ : Fin r) (_ : Fin d), SheafOfModules.unit R ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit R :=
306	    fun i' p => scalarEnd ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom ((universalMatrix d r I hI) p i'))
307	  (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit R)).symm.hom ≫
308	    biproduct.matrix M ≫
309	    (biproduct.isoCoproduct (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit R)).hom
310	
311	/-- The chart quotient `u^I` sends the `(I.orderIsoOfFin k)`-th basis section of
312	`O_{U^I}^r` to the `k`-th basis section of `O_{U^I}^d`. Project-local: the column-`I`
313	restriction of `u^I` is the identity, the matrix-level content of `lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`. -/
314	private lemma chartQuotientMap_ιFree (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
315	    (k : Fin d) :
316	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
317	        ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI k : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
318	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree k := by
319	  set A := CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) with hA
320	  set S := AlgebraicGeometry.Spec A with hS
321	  haveI : HasFiniteBiproducts (SheafOfModules S.ringCatSheaf) :=
322	    HasFiniteBiproducts.of_hasFiniteProducts
323	  change SheafOfModules.ιFree (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k)) ≫
324	      ((biproduct.isoCoproduct
325	            (fun _ : Fin r => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).symm.hom ≫
326	        biproduct.matrix (fun (i' : Fin r) (p : Fin d) => scalarEnd
327	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom (universalMatrix d r I hI p i'))) ≫
328	        (biproduct.isoCoproduct
329	            (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf)).hom)
330	      = SheafOfModules.ιFree k
331	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, SheafOfModules.ιFree, biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv]
332	  erw [Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
333	  rw [biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.isoCoproduct_hom]
334	  have h1 : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv) (1 : A) = 1 := map_one _
335	  have h0 : (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv) (0 : A) = 0 := map_zero _
336	  have hsub := universalMatrix_submatrix_self d r I hI
337	  have lift_eq :
338	      (biproduct.lift fun p : Fin d => scalarEnd
339	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso A).inv.hom (universalMatrix d r I hI p (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k)))))
340	        = biproduct.ι (fun _ : Fin d => SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) k := by
341	    refine biproduct.hom_ext _ _ (fun p => ?_)
342	    rw [biproduct.lift_π]
343	    have hentry : universalMatrix d r I hI p (↑((I.orderIsoOfFin hI) k))
344	        = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) A) p k :=
345	      congrFun (congrFun hsub p) k
346	    rw [hentry, Matrix.one_apply]
347	    by_cases hpk : p = k
348	    · rw [if_pos hpk, h1, scalarEnd_one, hpk, biproduct.ι_π_self]
349	    · rw [if_neg hpk, h0, scalarEnd_zero, biproduct.ι_π_ne _ (Ne.symm hpk)]
350	  rw [lift_eq, biproduct.ι_desc]
351	  rfl
352	
353	/-- **The chart quotient is an epimorphism** (`lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi`): `u^I` is split
354	by the coordinate inclusion `s_I` of the `I`-indexed columns, hence is a (split) epimorphism
355	of sheaves of modules. -/
356	lemma chartQuotientMap_epi (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
357	    Epi (chartQuotientMap d r I hI) := by
358	  have hsplit : SheafOfModules.freeMap (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf)
359	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫ chartQuotientMap d r I hI
360	      = 𝟙 (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) := by
361	    refine Cofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (SheafOfModules.isColimitFreeCofan (Fin d)) _ _ (fun k => ?_)
362	    have key : SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) k ≫
363	          (SheafOfModules.freeMap (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) ≫
364	            chartQuotientMap d r I hI)
365	        = SheafOfModules.ιFree k :=
366	      (SheafOfModules.ιFree_freeMap_assoc (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) k
367	        (chartQuotientMap d r I hI)).trans (chartQuotientMap_ιFree d r I hI k)
368	    exact key.trans (Category.comp_id _).symm
369	  exact (IsSplitEpi.mk' ⟨_, hsplit⟩).epi
370	
371	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
372	
373	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
374	
375	/-! ## The GL_d bundle transition cocycle
376	
377	The universal quotient `U` is glued from the per-chart free rank-`d` sheaves `O_{U^I}^d`
378	along the bundle transitions `g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)⁻¹`, realised as matrix automorphisms via
379	`matrixToFreeIso` and conjugated to the overlap pullbacks by `pullbackFreeIso`. This section
380	constructs `bundleTransition` and proves its self-identity (C1); the triple-overlap
381	multiplicativity (C2) is the matrix cocycle of `lem:gr_cocycle` transported to the common
382	overlap by `pullbackBaseChangeTransport`/`glueData_bridge_*`. -/
383	
384	/-- The Cramer inverse of the self-minor `X^I_I` is the identity: since `X^I_I = 1`
385	(`universalMatrix_submatrix_self`) its inverse is `1`. Project-local; underlies C1. -/
386	lemma universalMinorInv_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
387	    universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
388	  have hmin : universalMinor d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
389	    rw [universalMinor, universalMatrix_submatrix_self, Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)]
390	  rw [universalMinorInv, hmin, inv_one]
391	
392	/-- The injected Cramer inverse and minor matrices over the overlap structure sheaf are
393	mutually inverse — the `GL_d` invertibility hypotheses for `matrixToFreeIso`. Project-local. -/
394	private lemma bundleMatrix_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
395	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
396	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
397	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
398	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) *
399	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
400	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
401	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 ∧
402	    ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
403	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
404	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)) *
405	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
406	        (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
407	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ)) = 1 := by
408	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
409	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, map_one]
410	  · rw [← map_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2, map_one]
411	
412	/-- The **bundle transition** `g_{I,J}` (`def:gr_bundleTransition`): the isomorphism of
413	sheaves of modules on the overlap `U^I_J` induced by the invertible matrix
414	`(X^I_J)⁻¹ = universalMinorInv d r I J`. It identifies the pullback of `O_{U^I}^d` along
415	`f_{IJ}` with the pullback of `O_{U^J}^d` along `t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI}`, by conjugating the
416	matrix automorphism `matrixToFreeIso (X^I_J)⁻¹` (built like `chartQuotientMap`) by the
417	free-pullback comparisons `pullbackFreeIso`. -/
418	noncomputable def bundleTransition (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r))
419	    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) :
420	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)).obj
421	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) ≅
422	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)).obj
423	        (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
424	  Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (Fin d) ≪≫
425	    matrixToFreeIso
426	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
427	          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
428	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ))
429	      ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
430	          (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
431	        (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ))
432	      (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1
433	      (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I J hI hJ).2 ≪≫
434	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso
435	      (chartTransition d r I J hI hJ ≫ chartIncl d r J I hJ hI) (Fin d)).symm
436	
437	/-- **Self-identity of the bundle transition (C1)** (`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id`): on the
438	diagonal overlap `U^I_I` (where `t_{II} = 𝟙`) the bundle transition is the identity, in the
439	form required by the gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. The matrix part is the identity
440	since `(X^I_I)⁻¹ = 1` (`universalMinorInv_self`), so `matrixEnd 1 = 𝟙` (`matrixEnd_one`); the
441	two free-pullback comparisons then cancel into the `eqToIso` transport.
442	
443	Resource note (iter-060): the former `set_option maxHeartbeats 1000000 in` override is
444	removed and the proof rebuilt as a *leaner term* that the kernel checks within the default
445	budget (the earlier `.hom`-level cast chain hit a `(kernel) deterministic timeout` at default
446	heartbeats and an OOM ceiling on cold builds at `1000000`). The new term works at the **iso
447	level**: the matrix automorphism is collapsed to `Iso.refl` in the lightweight single-overlap
448	context (`hB`, free sheaves only — no pullback), and the two free-pullback comparisons cancel
449	through the *generic* lemma `pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso` (proved by `subst` on
450	variable morphisms), so the kernel never whnfs the concrete immersions `chartIncl` /
451	`chartTransition`. -/
452	theorem bundleTransition_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
453	    bundleTransition d r I I hI hI
454	      = eqToIso (congrArg
455	          (fun φ => (Scheme.Modules.pullback φ).obj
456	            (SheafOfModules.free (R := (affineChart d r I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)))
457	          (show chartIncl d r I I hI hI
458	              = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI from by
459	            rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp])) := by
460	  have hφ : chartIncl d r I I hI hI
461	      = chartTransition d r I I hI hI ≫ chartIncl d r I I hI hI := by
462	    rw [chartTransition_self, Category.id_comp]
463	  -- The matrix automorphism is the identity iso: `(X^I_I)⁻¹` injects to the identity matrix,
464	  -- so its `matrixEnd` is `𝟙`. Proved here over the single overlap chart (no pullback types).
465	  have hB : matrixToFreeIso
466	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
467	            (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
468	          (universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI))
469	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso
470	            (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
471	          (universalMinor d r I I hI hI))
472	        (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I I hI hI).1
473	        (bundleMatrix_cancel d r I I hI hI).2
474	      = Iso.refl _ := by
475	    apply Iso.ext
476	    rw [matrixToFreeIso_hom, Iso.refl_hom, universalMinorInv_self, map_one, matrixEnd_one]
477	  -- Unfold the transition, collapse the identity matrix factor (`erw` to bridge the
478	  -- `chartOverlap`/`Spec` defeq on the inferred base scheme), and cancel the comparisons.
479	  simp only [bundleTransition]
480	  erw [hB, Iso.refl_trans]
481	  exact Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso hφ (Fin d)
482	
483	/-- The bundle transition data packaged over the Grassmannian glue datum, ready to feed the
484	gluing primitive `Scheme.Modules.glue`. Project-local. -/
485	noncomputable def bundleTransitionData (d r : ℕ) :
486	    ∀ (I J : (theGlueData d r).J),
487	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).f I J)).obj
488	          (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) ≅
489	        (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).t I J ≫ (theGlueData d r).f J I)).obj
490	          (SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U J).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d)) :=
491	  fun I J => bundleTransition d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
492	
493	/-! ### The matrix-level Cramer-inverse cocycle (L1)
494	
495	The matrix-algebra core of (C2) is the Cramer-inverse cocycle
496	`(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹` over the triple-overlap ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`.
497	Its proof reduces to the image-matrix cocycle `cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` of
498	`GrassmannianCells` by taking the `I`-minor. That lemma and the matrix helpers it depends on
499	are `private` in `GrassmannianCells.lean`, so they are reproduced here as project-local
500	helpers (the proofs are verbatim ports of the known-good originals). -/
501	
502	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.mul_submatrix_col` (private there). -/
503	private lemma mul_submatrix_col' {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
504	    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
505	    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
506	  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]
507	
508	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.map_nonsing_inv` (private there). -/
509	private lemma map_nonsing_inv' {n : ℕ} {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S)
510	    (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) (h : IsUnit A.det) :
511	    (A.map f)⁻¹ = A⁻¹.map f := by
512	  have hmul : (A.map f) * (A⁻¹.map f) = 1 := by
513	    rw [← Matrix.map_mul, Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A h, Matrix.map_one f (map_zero f) (map_one f)]
514	  exact Matrix.inv_eq_right_inv hmul
515	
516	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.map_map_eq_of_comp` (private there). -/
517	private lemma map_map_eq_of_comp' {m n : ℕ} {R A D : Type*}
518	    [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing D]
519	    (M : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R) (f : R →+* A) (g : A →+* D) (h : R →+* D)
520	    (hcomp : g.comp f = h) : (M.map f).map g = M.map h := by
521	  rw [Matrix.map_map, ← RingHom.coe_comp, hcomp]
522	
523	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.isUnit_algebraMap_away_left` (private there). -/
524	private lemma isUnit_algebraMap_away_left' {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
525	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) x) := by
526	  have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
527	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
528	  rw [map_mul] at h
529	  exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_left h
530	
531	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel` (private there). -/
532	private lemma inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel' {d e : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
533	    (A B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin e) R) (hA : IsUnit A.det) :
534	    (B⁻¹ * A) * (A⁻¹ * M) = B⁻¹ * M := by
535	  rw [Matrix.mul_assoc B⁻¹ A (A⁻¹ * M), ← Matrix.mul_assoc A A⁻¹ M,
536	    Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A hA, Matrix.one_mul]
537	
538	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.imageMatrix_map_eq` (private there). -/
539	private lemma imageMatrix_map_eq' (d r : ℕ) (I X : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
540	    (hX : X.card = d) {D : Type*} [CommRing D]
541	    [Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D]
542	    (incl : Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX) →+* D)
543	    (hincl : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
544	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))
545	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) :
546	    (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
547	      = (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
548	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D)).submatrix id
549	          (fun j : Fin d => (X.orderIsoOfFin hX j : Fin r)))⁻¹ *
550	        (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
551	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) := by
552	  have hmm : (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
553	      = (universalMinorInv d r I X hI hX).map incl
554	        * ((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
555	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
556	              (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))).map incl := by
557	    rw [imageMatrix]; exact Matrix.map_mul
558	  rw [hmm, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hincl, universalMinorInv,
559	    ← map_nonsing_inv' incl (universalMinor d r I X hI hX)
560	        (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I X hI hX)]
561	  congr 1
562	  rw [universalMinor, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hincl, ← Matrix.submatrix_map]
563	
564	/-- Port of `GrassmannianCells.cocycle_imageMatrix_eq` (private there): over the triple
565	overlap `S_I`, the image matrix `(X^I_K)⁻¹ X^I` of `θ_{I,K}` equals `θ_{I,J}` applied
566	entrywise to the image matrix `(X^J_K)⁻¹ X^J` of `θ_{J,K}`. -/
567	private lemma cocycle_imageMatrix_eq' (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
568	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
569	    (imageMatrix d r I K hI hK).map
570	        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
571	      = (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
572	          ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
573	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))) := by
574	  have hLHS := imageMatrix_map_eq' d r I K hI hK
575	    (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))

639	/-- **Cramer-inverse cocycle on the triple overlap (L1)** (`lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix`):
640	over the triple-overlap ring `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]` the base-changed Cramer inverses of

642	`(X^J_K)⁻¹ (X^I_J)⁻¹ = (X^I_K)⁻¹`. This is the pure matrix-algebra core of (C2), independent
643	of any sheaf data. Project-local. -/
644	theorem bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r))
645	    (hI : I.card = d) (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
646	    (universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK).map
647	        ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
648	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
649	      * (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).map
650	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
651	      = (universalMinorInv d r I K hI hK).map
652	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
653	  -- Take the `I`-minor (columns indexed by `I`) of the image-matrix cocycle.
654	  have hcol := congrArg
655	    (fun M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away
656	        (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK)) =>
657	      M.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)))
658	    (cocycle_imageMatrix_eq' d r I J K hI hJ hK)
659	  simp only at hcol
660	  -- LHS of `hcol` is `(X^I_K)⁻¹` over `S_I`.
661	  rw [Matrix.submatrix_map, imageMatrix_submatrix_I] at hcol
662	  -- RHS of `hcol`: push the `I`-minor through the outer map.
663	  rw [Matrix.submatrix_map] at hcol
664	  -- `imageMatrix J K = (X^J_K)⁻¹ * X^J`, so its `I`-minor splits off the inverse factor;
665	  -- the second factor is `X^J` (over `R^J[1/P^J_K]`) restricted to the `I`-columns.
666	  have hsplit : (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).submatrix id
667	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
668	      = universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK *
669	        ((universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
670	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
671	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).submatrix id
672	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) := by
673	    rw [imageMatrix]; exact mul_submatrix_col' _ _ _
674	  rw [hsplit, Matrix.map_mul] at hcol
675	  -- The comp identity `θ_{I,J}` realises the cross-localisation map on `R^J`.
676	  have hχ : ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
677	        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))).comp
678	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
679	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
680	      = (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
681	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom := by
682	    rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap, cocycleΘIJ]
683	    exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp _ _
684	  have e1 : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
685	        ((awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
686	          (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
687	      = (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).map
688	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
689	    rw [← map_map_eq_of_comp' (universalMatrix d r J hJ)
690	        (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom
691	        (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) _ rfl]
692	    congr 1
693	    exact universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ
694	  -- The base change of `X^J|_I` over `θ_{I,J}` is `(X^I_J)⁻¹` over `S_I`.
695	  have hXJI : (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).map
696	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
697	            (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).submatrix id
698	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map
699	          ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
700	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
701	      = (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).map
702	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) := by
703	    rw [Matrix.submatrix_map, map_map_eq_of_comp' _ _ _ _ hχ, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, e1,
704	      Matrix.submatrix_map, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
705	  rw [hXJI] at hcol
706	  exact hcol.symm

708	/-! ### L3 transport: `scalarEnd`/`matrixEnd` naturality under pullback
709	
710	The substantive new infrastructure for (C2). The atom is `scalarEnd_pullback`: pulling back a
711	scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` along a scheme morphism `p` is, after the unit-pullback
712	comparison `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, the scalar endomorphism of the base-changed function
713	`p.appTop a`. Its proof transposes the naturality square under the pullback-pushforward
714	adjunction to a `unit`-level identity, which holds by naturality of the comorphism `p.c`. -/
715	
716	/-- The reduced (transposed) form of the scalar-naturality atom: on the unit sheaf,
717	multiplication by `a` followed by the comorphism `unitToPushforwardObjUnit` equals the
718	comorphism followed by the pushforward of multiplication by `p.appTop a`. Project-local
719	helper for `scalarEnd_pullback`. -/
720	lemma unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) (a : Γ(S, ⊤)) :
721	    scalarEnd a ≫ SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
722	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) ≫
723	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map (scalarEnd (p.appTop a)) := by
724	  apply ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).obj
725	    (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)).unitHomEquiv.injective
726	  refine PresheafOfModules.sections_ext _ _ (fun Y => ?_)
727	  -- Both `.val Y` are nested applications (no morphism composite) up to defeq, since
728	  -- `unitHomEquiv (f ≫ p) = sectionsMap p (unitHomEquiv f)` and `sectionsMap`/`unitHomEquiv`
729	  -- are `rfl`/`sectionsMk`-defined; rewrite into that form via `change`.
730	  change (SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)).val.app Y
731	        ((scalarEnd a).val.app Y (1 : S.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
732	      = ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map
733	            (scalarEnd ((Scheme.Hom.appTop p) a))).val.app Y
734	        ((SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)).val.app Y
735	          (1 : S.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj Y))
736	  rw [scalarEnd_val_app_one, SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply,
737	    SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit_val_app_apply, map_one]
738	  -- Goal: `φ.hom.app Y (a|_Y) = ((pushforward p).map (scalarEnd (p.appTop a))).val.app Y 1`.
739	  -- RHS reduces (defeq, the pushforward's `map`-application is `rfl` + `scalarEnd_val_app_one`)
740	  -- to `T.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (p.appTop a)`; LHS rewrites to the same by
741	  -- naturality of the comorphism `(toRingCatSheafHom p).hom` at `(homOfLE le_top).op : op ⊤ ⟶ Y`.
742	  have hnat := ConcreteCategory.congr_hom
743	    ((Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p).hom.naturality (homOfLE (le_top : Y.unop ≤ ⊤)).op) a
744	  rw [CategoryTheory.comp_apply, CategoryTheory.comp_apply] at hnat
745	  rw [hnat]
746	  -- The RHS pushforward (its `map`-application is `rfl` on sections) evaluates the
747	  -- scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd (p.appTop a)` at `1` over the preimage open; both sides are
748	  -- then `T.ringCatSheaf.obj.map (homOfLE le_top).op (p.appTop a)` (the `homOfLE`s agree by
749	  -- proof irrelevance, the comorphism by `forget₂`-on-elements), so `scalarEnd_val_app_one` closes.
750	  exact (scalarEnd_val_app_one ((Scheme.Hom.appTop p) a)
751	    (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map p.base).obj (Opposite.unop Y)))).symm
752	
753	/-- **ATOM: scalar endomorphism naturality under pullback** (`lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback`).
754	For `p : T ⟶ S` and `a ∈ Γ(S,⊤)`, pulling back the scalar endomorphism `scalarEnd a` is,
755	after the unit-pullback comparison `q = pullbackObjUnitToUnit`, the scalar endomorphism of the
756	base-changed function `p.appTop a`:
757	`(pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) ≫ q = q ≫ scalarEnd (p.appTop a)`.
758	Proved by transposing under the pullback-pushforward adjunction to
759	`unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm`.
760	Project-local — the single irreducible new claim underlying `matrixEnd_pullback`. -/
761	lemma scalarEnd_pullback {T S : Scheme.{0}} (p : T ⟶ S) (a : Γ(S, ⊤)) :
762	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback p).map (scalarEnd a) ≫
763	        SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p)
764	      = SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) ≫
765	        scalarEnd (p.appTop a) := by
766	  apply (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).homEquiv
767	    (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf) |>.injective
768	  -- `homEquiv_naturality_left` collapses the LHS transpose; the RHS transpose
769	  -- (`homEquiv_naturality_right`) is supplied as a term because positional `rw` cannot match
770	  -- the identical-printing `homEquiv` under the `X.Modules` diamond (memory
771	  -- `grquot-functor-dropped-termmode`).
772	  -- `hq` is the lemma `..._homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit` restated in the `Scheme.Modules`
773	  -- adjunction form (defeq to the `SheafOfModules` form), so `rw`/`congrArg` match syntactically.
774	  have hq : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).homEquiv
775	        (SheafOfModules.unit S.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit T.ringCatSheaf)
776	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p))
777	      = SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p) :=
778	    SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit _
779	  rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left, hq]
780	  refine (unitToPushforward_scalarEnd_comm p a).trans ?_
781	  exact (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p).homEquiv_naturality_right
782	        (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom p))
783	        (scalarEnd (p.appTop a))).trans
784	      (congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p).map (scalarEnd (p.appTop a))) hq)).symm
785	
786	/-- The action of `matrixEnd M` on the `j`-th free injection: `ιFree j ≫ matrixEnd M`
787	expands as the sum over rows `∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ ιFree k`. Project-local helper
788	for `matrixEnd_pullback`. -/
789	lemma ιFree_matrixEnd {S : Scheme.{0}} {d : ℕ} (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) Γ(S, ⊤))
790	    (j : Fin d) :
791	    SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) j ≫ matrixEnd M
792	      = ∑ k, scalarEnd (M k j) ≫ SheafOfModules.ιFree (R := S.ringCatSheaf) k := by
793	  rw [matrixEnd, SheafOfModules.ιFree]
794	  simp only [SheafOfModules.free]
795	  rw [Iso.symm_hom, biproduct.isoCoproduct_inv, biproduct.isoCoproduct_hom,
796	    ← Category.assoc, Sigma.ι_desc, biproduct.ι_matrix_assoc, biproduct.lift_desc]
797	  rfl
