1342	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1343	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
1344	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L903-L910).
1345	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1346	  % "Compatible with the co-cycle $(\theta_{I,J})$
1347	  %  for gluing the affine pieces $U^I$, we give gluing data
1348	  %  $(g_{I,J})$ for gluing together the trivial bundles $\oplus^d\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I}$
1349	  %  by putting
1350	  %  $$g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in GL_d(U^I_J)$$"
1351	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
1352	  Over each overlap \(U^I_J = \Spec R^I_J\) define the bundle-transition matrix
1353	  \[
1354	    g_{I,J} \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} \;\in\; \mathrm{GL}_d(R^I_J),
1355	  \]
1356	  the Cramer inverse of the localised \(J\)-minor (\cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}),
1357	  which is invertible because \(\det(X^I_J)\) is a unit of \(R^I_J\). The matrices
1358	  \((g_{I,J})\) are compatible with the chart-gluing cocycle \((\theta_{I,J})\)
1359	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{lem:gr_cocycle}): over a triple overlap they
1360	  satisfy \(g_{I,K} = g_{J,K}\, g_{I,J}\) and \(g_{I,I} = 1\), the multiplicative
1361	  cocycle condition for a vector bundle. Gluing the free rank-\(d\) sheaves
1362	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\) along the isomorphisms \(g_{I,J}\) over the cover
1363	  \(\{U^I\}\) of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) therefore
1364	  produces a locally free \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}\)-module
1365	  \(\mathcal{U}\) of rank \(d\). Its determinant line bundle \(\det \mathcal{U}\)

1367	  Pl\"ucker embedding.
1368	\end{definition}

1370	The tautological quotient \(u\) is assembled from the chart quotients \(u^I\) by the
1371	section-level descent \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom}; its one remaining content is the
1372	\emph{overlap-compatibility} of the family \((u^I)\) with the bundle gluing
1373	\((g_{I,J})\). The next four blocks isolate that compatibility: a rectangular analogue of
1374	the square matrix-endomorphism API (\cref{def:gr_matrixEnd},
1375	\cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}) for the
1376	\(r \to d\) chart quotient (\cref{def:gr_matrixEndRect},
1377	\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}), the
1378	adjunction transposition that turns the descent condition into a pullback-level identity
1379	(\cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose}), and the matrix-core assembly
1380	that closes it (\cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}).

1385	[Rectangular matrix homomorphism of free sheaves]
1386	  \label{def:gr_matrixEndRect}

1388	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
1389	  Let \(S\) be a scheme, \(d, r \in \mathbb{N}\), and
1390	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times r}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) a rectangular matrix
1391	  of global functions. The \emph{rectangular matrix homomorphism}
1392	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) is the morphism of free sheaves
1393	  \[
1394	    \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M) \;:\; \mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}
1395	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}
1396	  \]
1397	  whose \((k,\ell)\)-component -- \(k \in \{1,\dots,d\}\) an output index,
1398	  \(\ell \in \{1,\dots,r\}\) an input index -- under the presentation of
1399	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}\) and \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) as finite biproducts of copies of the
1400	  unit module \(\mathbf 1\), is the scalar endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{k,\ell})\)
1401	  (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}). It is assembled from these components by the universal property
1402	  of the two biproducts, exactly as the square \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\)
1403	  (\cref{def:gr_matrixEnd}) and the chart quotient (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}). When
1404	  \(r = d\) it specialises to \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\); and the chart quotient \(u^I\)
1405	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) is by construction \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(X^I)\) of the
1406	  universal \(d \times r\) matrix \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}).
1407	\end{definition}

1409	\begin{lemma}
1410	
1411	\leanok
1412	[Rectangular matrix homomorphism is natural under pullback]
1413	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}
1414	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEndRect_pullback}
1415	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback,

1417	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and
1418	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(S,\mathcal{O}_S))\), with entrywise
1419	  base-changed matrix \(p^{\sharp}M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(\Gamma(T,\mathcal{O}_T))\).
1420	  Write \(Q_r : p^{*}\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\) and
1421	  \(Q_d : p^{*}\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) for the
1422	  free-pullback comparisons (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) at the index sets
1423	  \(\{1,\dots,r\}\) and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). Then the pullback of
1424	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}(M)\) is conjugate, through \(Q_r\) and \(Q_d\), to the
1425	  rectangular homomorphism of the base-changed matrix:
1426	  \[

1428	      \;=\; Q_d^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(p^{\sharp}M) \,\circ\, Q_r .

1432	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEndRect, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,

1434	  Identical to the square case \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}, with the source and target
1435	  biproducts now indexed by \(\{1,\dots,r\}\) and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\) respectively. The
1436	  pullback functor \(p^{*}\), a left adjoint, is additive and biproduct-preserving; through
1437	  the coproduct comparisons underlying the free-pullback isomorphisms
1438	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} it carries the biproduct presentation of
1439	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,r}\) (resp. \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\)) to that of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)
1440	  (resp. \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\)), the reassociation of the iterated pullbacks being
1441	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. On each single \((k,\ell)\)-component the pulled-back
1442	  scalar endomorphism is, by \cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}, the comparison-conjugate of
1443	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}((p^{\sharp}M)_{k,\ell})\). Reassembling over the two biproducts, the

1503	  intertwines the pullbacks of \(u^I\) and \(u^J\) over \(U^I_J\) -- is equivalent, under the
1504	  pullback--pushforward adjunction of the overlap immersion, to the pullback-level identity
1505	  of morphisms \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\)
1506	  \[
1507	    g_{I,J} \,\circ\, f_{IJ}^{*}\,u^I
1508	      \;=\; (t_{IJ} \circ f_{JI})^{*}\,u^J,
1509	  \]
1510	  understood through the free-pullback comparisons (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) on
1511	  source and target.

1513	\begin{proof}

1515	    lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp}

1517	  quotients; the descent condition of \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom} is stated on those
1518	  transposes. Transposing back across the adjunction is the naturality of the conjugate
1519	  (mate) construction against the hom-equivalences, \cref{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app},
1520	  together with the compatibility of the conjugate with the iterated-pullback comparison
1521	  \cref{lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib} reassociating the two pullbacks
1522	  \(f_{IJ}^{*}\) and \((t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*}\) via \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. The
1523	  free-pullback comparisons \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} re-present both sides as morphisms
1524	  of the trivial bundles \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\), under
1525	  which the transposed condition becomes the displayed pullback-level identity.
1526	\end{proof}

1541	  section-level descent \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom} for the bundle gluing \((g_{I,J})\):
1542	  the pullback-level identity \(g_{I,J} \circ f_{IJ}^{*} u^I = (t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*} u^J\)

1545	\begin{proof}
1546	  \uses{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose, lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback,
1547	    lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp, def:gr_bundleTransition,
1548	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_eq,
1549	    lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left}

1551	  pullback-level identity \(g_{I,J} \circ f_{IJ}^{*} u^I = (t_{IJ}\circ f_{JI})^{*} u^J\) on
1552	  \(U^I_J\). Both sides are morphisms of trivial bundles \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,r} \to
1553	  \mathcal{O}_{U^I_J}^{\,d}\); write each as \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) of a \(d \times r\)
1554	  matrix over the overlap ring \(R^I_J\) and compare matrices.
1555	
1556	  Since \(u^I = \mathrm{matrixEndRect}(X^I)\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}), the
1557	  base-change naturality \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback} identifies \(f_{IJ}^{*} u^I\),
1558	  through the free-pullback comparisons, with \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}\) of the localised
1559	  matrix \(X^I\) over \(R^I_J\) (the global-sections base change is the away-localisation
1560	  \cref{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left}). The bundle transition \(g_{I,J}\) is
1561	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\) of the Cramer inverse \((X^I_J)^{-1}\)
1562	  (\cref{def:gr_bundleTransition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}); hence by the
1563	  square-after-rectangular composition law \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp},
1564	  \[
1565	    g_{I,J} \circ f_{IJ}^{*} u^I
1566	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEndRect}\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} \cdot X^I\bigr).
1567	  \]

1569	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap}, transported across the overlap by
1570	  \cref{lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_eq}) turns the right-hand matrix into \(X^J\). Symmetrically,
1571	  \(\mathrm{matrixEndRect}_J\) of \(X^J\) is, by \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback} along

1573	  the two sides agree, which is the required overlap compatibility.

1576	\begin{definition}
1577	
1578	\leanok
1579	[The tautological quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}^{r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\)]
1580	  \label{def:tautological_quotient}
1581	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.tautologicalQuotient}
1582	  \uses{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:gr_chart_quotient,
1583	    def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle,
1584	    def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
1585	    def:gr_modules_glueHom, lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}
1586	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1587	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
1588	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L908-L910).
1589	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1590	  % "This is compatible with the homomorphisms $u^I$,
1591	  %  so we get a surjective homomorphism
1592	  %  $u : \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$."
1593	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
1594	  The chart quotients \(u^I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
1595	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) are compatible with the bundle gluing
1596	  \((g_{I,J})\) of \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}): over
1597	  each overlap \(U^I_J\) one has the identity of \(d \times r\) matrices
1598	  \[
1599	    g_{I,J}\, X^I \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I \;=\; X^J \qquad \text{on } U^I_J,
1600	  \]
1601	  which is precisely the defining formula of the chart transition
1602	  \(\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}, \cref{def:gr_universalMinorInv}). At the
1603	  module level this overlap compatibility -- the equalising condition consumed by the
1604	  section-level descent \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom} -- is exactly
1605	  \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotient_overlap}, whose proof routes the matrix identity above
1606	  through the rectangular pullback/composition API
1607	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_pullback}, \cref{lem:gr_matrixEndRect_comp}) and the
1608	  adjunction transpose \cref{lem:gr_tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose}.
1609	  Hence the local maps \(u^I\), transported through the trivialisations of
1610	  \(\mathcal{U}\), agree on overlaps and glue to a single surjective
1611	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}\)-linear homomorphism
1612	  \[
1613	    u \;:\; \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r}
1614	      \;\twoheadrightarrow\; \mathcal{U},
1615	  \]
1616	  the \emph{tautological} (or \emph{universal}) rank-\(d\) quotient of the trivial
1617	  rank-\(r\) sheaf. It is surjective because each \(u^I\) is (split) surjective and
1618	  surjectivity is local on \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), and \(\mathcal{U}\) is locally
1619	  free of rank \(d\) by \cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}. The pair
1620	  \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\) is the family of rank-\(d\) quotients carried
1621	  by \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) itself.
1622	\end{definition}

1624	\section{The functor of points}
1625	\label{sec:grquot_functor}
1626	
1627	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme \(T\) to the set of \emph{equivalence classes} of
1628	rank-\(d\) quotients of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). The following block packages a single such
1629	quotient, the equivalence relation on them, and the pullback action through which the
1630	functor acts on morphisms; the two coherence blocks after it isolate the comparison
1631	identities that make that action functorial.
1632	
1633	\begin{definition}[Rank-\(d\) quotients and their pullback action]
1634	  \label{def:gr_rankQuotient}
1635	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient,
1636	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.Rel,
1637	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_refl,
1638	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_symm,
1639	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.RankQuotient.rel_trans,
1640	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqSetoid,
1641	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback,
1642	        AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.rqPullback_rel}
1643	  \uses{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1644	        def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
1645	  A \emph{rank-\(d\) quotient of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)} on a scheme \(T\) is a triple
1646	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) consisting of a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-modules
1647	  \(\mathcal{F}\) that is locally free of rank \(d\) (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank})
1648	  together with an epimorphism \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) out
1649	  of the trivial rank-\(r\) bundle. Two such quotients \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\)
1650	  and \(y = \langle \mathcal{F}', q'\rangle\) are \emph{equivalent}, written \(x \sim y\),
1651	  when there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with
1652	  \(q \circ f = q'\) (equivalently \(\ker q = \ker q'\)). This relation is reflexive
1653	  (\(f = \mathrm{id}\)), symmetric (\(f \mapsto f^{-1}\)), and transitive (composition of the
1654	  witnessing isomorphisms), hence an equivalence relation -- the setoid whose quotient is the
1655	  functor's value at \(T\).
1656	
1657	  For a morphism \(\psi : T' \to T\) the \emph{pullback action} sends
1658	  \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) to
1659	  \[
1660	    \psi^{*}x \;=\;
1661	      \bigl\langle\, \psi^{*}\mathcal{F},\;
1662	        \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,r} \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
1663	        \psi^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\bigr) \xrightarrow{\ \psi^{*}q\ }
1664	        \psi^{*}\mathcal{F} \,\bigr\rangle,
1665	  \]
1666	  re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via the free-pullback isomorphism
1667	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}. The result is again a rank-\(d\) quotient: the composite is
1668	  an epimorphism because pullback, a left adjoint, preserves epimorphisms and the
1669	  re-presentation is an isomorphism, and \(\psi^{*}\mathcal{F}\) is locally free of rank \(d\)
1670	  by \cref{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}. The pullback action respects the equivalence
1671	  relation -- given \(f\) witnessing \(x \sim y\), the pulled-back isomorphism \(\psi^{*}f\)
1672	  witnesses \(\psi^{*}x \sim \psi^{*}y\), by functoriality of pullback -- so it descends to a
1673	  map of equivalence classes.
1674	\end{definition}
1675	
1676	The two blocks below record the comparison identities, between the free-pullback isomorphism
1677	\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} and Mathlib's pseudofunctor structure on pullback, that make
1678	the descended pullback action of \cref{def:gr_rankQuotient} functorial.
1679	
1680	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1681	[Identity coherence of the free-pullback comparison]
1682	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1683	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1684	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1685	  For the identity morphism \(\mathrm{id}_T\), the canonical comparison morphism of unit
1686	  modules \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*}\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\) -- the map underlying the
1687	  free-pullback isomorphism \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index -- coincides
1688	  with the component at \(\mathbf{1}\) of the pullback-identity isomorphism
1689	  \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*} \cong \mathrm{id}\).
1690	\end{lemma}
1691	
1692	\begin{proof}
1693	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1694	  The comparison morphism is the mate of \(\mathrm{id}_{\mathbf 1}\) under the
1695	  pullback--pushforward adjunction: the image of the identity of \(\mathbf 1\) through the
1696	  adjunction's hom-equivalence on the unit. For the identity scheme morphism the pushforward
1697	  is the identity functor, so the adjunction unit is itself an identity; Mathlib's
1698	  pullback-identity isomorphism is exactly the conjugate (left-adjoint transpose) of the
1699	  pushforward-identity isomorphism. Tracing the identity through the hom-equivalence and the
1700	  conjugate-transpose identity for the pullback-identity isomorphism shows the comparison
1701	  equals the \(\mathbf 1\)-component of that isomorphism.
1702	\end{proof}
1703	
1704	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1705	[Identity coherence at the free sheaf]
1706	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_id}
1707	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_id}
1708	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1709	  For the identity morphism \(\mathrm{id}_T\) and an index set \(I\), the free-pullback
1710	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*}(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1) \cong \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\) coincides
1711	  with the component at the free sheaf \(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\) of the pullback-identity
1712	  isomorphism \(\mathrm{id}_T^{*} \cong \mathrm{id}\).
1713	\end{lemma}
1714	
1715	\begin{proof}
1716	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}
1717	  Both sides are cocones on the coproduct \(\bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\); by coproduct
1718	  extensionality it suffices to check equality after precomposing with each coprojection
1719	  \(\mathbf 1 \to \bigoplus_I \mathbf 1\), which reduces to the one-element case
1720	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}.
1721	\end{proof}
1722	
1723	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1724	[Composite coherence of the free-pullback comparison]
1725	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1726	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}
1727	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1728	  For composable scheme morphisms \(a : T'' \to T'\) and \(b : T' \to T\), the comparison
1729	  morphism of unit modules \((b \circ a)^{*}\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\) factors as
1730	  \(a^{*}\) of the comparison for \(b\), the comparison for \(a\), and the component at
1731	  \(\mathbf{1}\) of the iterated-pullback comparison \(a^{*}b^{*} \cong (b\circ a)^{*}\)
1732	  (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}).

1735	\begin{proof}
1736	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id, def:modules_pullbackComp,
1737	    lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}
1738	  This is the composite analogue of \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id}: the mate
1739	  construction is compatible with composition of adjunctions, so the comparison for
1740	  \(b \circ a\) is the pasting of the comparisons for \(a\) and \(b\) reassociated by the
1741	  pseudofunctor comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. The compatibility of the mate
1742	  with the adjunction hom-equivalences is supplied by
1743	  \cref{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}, the reusable conjugate-naturality engine. The
1744	  pentagon coherence of the iterated-pullback comparisons makes the factorisation
1745	  well-defined.

1748	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1749	[Mate/conjugate compatibility of the adjunction hom-equivalence]
1750	  \label{lem:gr_homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}
1751	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homEquiv_conjugateEquiv_app}

1757	  construction against the two hom-equivalences; it is the reusable engine behind
1758	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp}.

1761	\begin{proof}
1762

1764	  the equality is the standard compatibility of the mate of \(\alpha\) with the
1765	  hom-set bijections.

2223	/-- The **tautological quotient** `u : O^r ↠ U` (`def:tautological_quotient`): the global
2224	surjection assembled from the chart quotients `u^I` (`chartQuotientMap`), compatible with
2225	the bundle gluing of `universalQuotient`. Since `universalQuotient` is the descent
2226	equalizer of pushforwards, the morphism is `glueLift` of the per-chart adjoint
2227	transposes (`tautologicalQuotientComponent`); the equalizing condition — the chart
2228	compatibility `g_{IJ} ∘ f_{IJ}^* u^I = (t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI})^* u^J`, whose matrix content is
2229	`X^J = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (`universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap` / `imageMatrix`) — is
2230	`tautologicalQuotient_overlap`, transposed through the adjunction by
2231	`tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose`. -/
2232	noncomputable def tautologicalQuotient (d r : ℕ) :
2233	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ universalQuotient d r :=
2234	  Scheme.Modules.glueLift (theGlueData d r)
2235	    (fun I => SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d))
2236	    (bundleTransitionData d r)
2237	    (fun I => bundleTransition_self d r I.1 I.2)
2238	    (fun I J K => bundleTransition_cocycle d r I J K)
2239	    (fun I => tautologicalQuotientComponent d r I)
2240	    (fun p => (tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose d r p.1 p.2).mpr
2241	      (tautologicalQuotient_overlap d r p.1 p.2))

2243	/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property
2244	
2245	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
2246	rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
2247	such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
2248	(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
2249	at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
2250	together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
2251	pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.
2252	
2253	Because a sheaf of modules `F : T.Modules` is a large object, this quotient lives in
2254	`Type 1` (not `Type 0` as the original scaffold signature stated); the corrected universe
2255	is the only change to the pinned signature, and it is forced — the substantive content is
2256	unchanged. -/
2257	
2258	/-- A **rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`** on a scheme `T`: a sheaf of modules `F` on `T`,
2259	locally free of rank `d`, together with an epimorphism `q : O_T^r ↠ F`. This is the
2260	unbundled datum whose equivalence classes form the value of the Grassmannian functor. -/
2261	structure RankQuotient (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) where
2262	  /-- The quotient sheaf. -/
2263	  F : T.Modules
2264	  /-- The quotient map out of the trivial rank-`r` bundle. -/
2265	  q : SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ F
2266	  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism (surjective). -/
2267	  epi : Epi q
2268	  /-- The quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`. -/
2269	  locFree : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank F d
2270	
2271	/-- Two rank-`d` quotients are **equivalent** when there is an isomorphism of the targets
2272	commuting with the quotient maps (equivalently, when the kernels of the quotient maps
2273	coincide). -/
2274	def RankQuotient.Rel {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x y : RankQuotient r d T) : Prop :=
2275	  ∃ f : x.F ≅ y.F, x.q ≫ f.hom = y.q
2276	
2277	lemma RankQuotient.rel_refl {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x : RankQuotient r d T) : x.Rel x :=
2278	  ⟨Iso.refl _, Category.comp_id _⟩
2279	
2280	lemma RankQuotient.rel_symm {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y : RankQuotient r d T}
2281	    (h : x.Rel y) : y.Rel x := by
2282	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
2283	  exact ⟨f.symm, by rw [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq]; exact hf.symm⟩
2284	
2285	lemma RankQuotient.rel_trans {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y z : RankQuotient r d T}
2286	    (h1 : x.Rel y) (h2 : y.Rel z) : x.Rel z := by
2287	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h1; obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h2
2288	  -- term-mode (the `T.Modules` def-diamond blocks positional category `rw`)
2289	  exact ⟨f ≪≫ g,
2290	    (congrArg (x.q ≫ ·) (Iso.trans_hom f g)).trans <|
2291	      (Category.assoc x.q f.hom g.hom).symm.trans <|
2292	        (congrArg (· ≫ g.hom) hf).trans hg⟩

2295	instance rqSetoid (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) : Setoid (RankQuotient r d T) where
2296	  r := RankQuotient.Rel
2297	  iseqv := ⟨RankQuotient.rel_refl, RankQuotient.rel_symm, RankQuotient.rel_trans⟩

2299	/-- The **pullback action** on a rank-`d` quotient: pull the target sheaf and quotient map
2300	back along `ψ`, re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via `pullbackFreeIso`. The
2301	result is again an epimorphism (pullback preserves epis) onto a rank-`d` locally free sheaf
2302	(`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank`). -/
2303	noncomputable def rqPullback {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
2304	    (x : RankQuotient r d T) : RankQuotient r d T' where
2305	  F := (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).obj x.F
2306	  q := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q
2307	  epi :=
2308	    -- fully explicit: the def-diamond on `T.Modules` blocks `Epi`-instance search, so
2309	    -- `x.epi` is threaded through `map_epi`/`epi_comp` by hand
2310	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
2311	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv inferInstance
2312	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q)
2313	      (@CategoryTheory.Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ) inferInstance _ _
2314	        x.q x.epi)
2315	  locFree := Scheme.Modules.pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank ψ x.locFree

2317	/-- The pullback action respects the equivalence relation, hence descends to quotients. -/
2318	lemma rqPullback_rel {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
2319	    {x y : RankQuotient r d T} (h : x.Rel y) :
2320	    (rqPullback ψ x).Rel (rqPullback ψ y) := by
2321	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
2322	  refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).mapIso f, ?_⟩
2323	  change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q) ≫
2324	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map f.hom
2325	    = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map y.q
2326	  rw [Category.assoc, ← (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map_comp]
2327	  exact congrArg
2328	    (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map m) hf
2329

2332	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

2334	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(r,d)` (`def:grassmannian_functor`): the
2335	contravariant functor from schemes to sets sending `T` to the set of equivalence classes
2336	of rank-`d` locally free quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F`, acting on morphisms by pullback.
2337	
2338	The object and morphism assignments are complete; the functoriality laws (`map_id`,
2339	`map_comp`) are discharged — via the naturality of the pseudofunctor comparison isomorphisms
2340	`pullbackId`/`pullbackComp` of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` — through the free-sheaf coherences
2341	`pullbackFreeIso_id`/`pullbackFreeIso_comp`, which reduce by coproduct extensionality to the
2342	unit-level coherences `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`/`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`. Fully proved. -/
2343	noncomputable def functor (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.{0}ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1 where
2344	  obj T := Quotient (rqSetoid r d T.unop)
2345	  map {X Y} g := TypeCat.ofHom (Quotient.map (rqPullback (r := r) (d := d) g.unop)
2346	    (fun _ _ h => rqPullback_rel g.unop h))
2347	  map_id X := by
2348	    -- reduce to the equivalence relation on representatives
2349	    ext z
2350	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
2351	    | _ x =>
2352	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (𝟙 X).unop x) = Quotient.mk _ x
2353	      -- the canonical iso `(𝟙)^* x.F ≅ x.F` is `pullbackId`; the quotient-map equation it
2354	      -- must satisfy reduces, by naturality of `pullbackId.hom`, to the free coherence
2355	      -- `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app (free _)`, hence to the unit-level identity
2356	      -- `pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app unit`. That coherence between
2357	      -- `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` and Mathlib's `pullbackId` is the open obstacle.
2358	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).app x.F, ?_⟩
2359	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (𝟙) x).q` and `(pullbackId.app x.F).hom` (defeq)
2360	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 X.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
2361	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X.unop)).map x.q) ≫
2362	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.app x.F = x.q
2363	      rw [Category.assoc, (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.naturality x.q,
2364	        ← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_id]
2365	      -- `(𝟭).map x.q = x.q` is only defeq, so close by term (rw can't see through it)
2366	      exact Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc _ _
2367	  map_comp {X Y Z} f g := by
2368	    ext z
2369	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
2370	    | _ x =>
2371	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (f ≫ g).unop x)
2372	        = Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x))
2373	      -- the canonical iso `(g.unop ≫ f.unop)^* x.F ≅ g.unop^*(f.unop^* x.F)` is `pullbackComp`;
2374	      -- the quotient-map equation reduces, by naturality, to the composite free coherence
2375	      -- relating `pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop)` to `pullbackFreeIso g.unop`/`f.unop`
2376	      -- through `pullbackComp` — the composite analogue of the `map_id` obstacle.
2377	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).app x.F).symm, ?_⟩
2378	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (g∘f) x).q` and `(pullbackComp.app x.F).symm.hom` (defeq), writing
2379	      -- the composite as `g.unop ≫ f.unop` so the `pullbackComp` naturality matches syntactically
2380	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
2381	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (g.unop ≫ f.unop)).map x.q) ≫
2382	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.app x.F
2383	        = (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x)).q
2384	      -- expose the `pullbackComp.inv` naturality square (mirrors the `map_id` reduction)
2385	      rw [Category.assoc, (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.naturality x.q]
2386	      -- the composite free coherence (`pullbackFreeIso_comp`) in inverse form: invert both
2387	      -- sides of the iso equation `pullbackComp.hom.app free ≫ pfba.hom = (pullback g).map pfa.hom
2388	      -- ≫ pfb.hom`.
2389	      have hstar : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
2390	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.app (SheafOfModules.free (Fin r))
2391	          = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso g.unop (Fin r)).inv ≫
2392	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop).map
2393	              (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso f.unop (Fin r)).inv := by
2394	        have hH := Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp f.unop g.unop (Fin r)
2395	        rw [← cancel_epi ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).hom.app
2396	          (SheafOfModules.free (Fin r)) ≫
2397	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).hom)]
2398	        trans (𝟙 _)
2399	        · rw [Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
2400	          exact (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).hom_inv_id_app _
2401	        · rw [hH]; simp
2402	      -- whisker `hstar` by `≫ (pullback f ⋙ pullback g).map x.q` and refold the RHS via
2403	      -- `map_comp` into `(rqPullback g (rqPullback f x)).q`.
2404	      exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
2405	        ((congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback f.unop ⋙
2406	              Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop).map x.q) hstar).trans
2407	          ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans
2408	            (congrArg ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso g.unop (Fin r)).inv ≫ ·)
2409	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop).map_comp
2410	                (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso f.unop (Fin r)).inv
2411	                ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f.unop).map x.q)).symm)))

2413	/-- **Chart restriction of the universal quotient sheaf**: over the `I`-th chart, the
2414	universal bundle `U` restricts to the free rank-`d` sheaf — the instantiation of the
2415	descent restriction isomorphism `Scheme.Modules.glueRestrictionIso` at the Grassmannian
2416	glue data. (Its underlying morphism is the adjoint transpose of the `I`-th
2417	descent-equalizer projection; iso-ness rides on
2418	`Scheme.Modules.isIso_glueRestrictionHom`.) -/
2419	noncomputable def universalQuotient_restrictionIso (d r : ℕ) (I : (theGlueData d r).J) :
2420	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι I)).obj (universalQuotient d r)
2421	      ≅ SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) :=
2422	  Scheme.Modules.glueRestrictionIso (theGlueData d r)
2423	    (fun I => SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d))
2424	    (bundleTransitionData d r)
2425	    (fun I => bundleTransition_self d r I.1 I.2)
2426	    (fun I J K => bundleTransition_cocycle d r I J K) I

2428	/-- **The universal quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`**
2429	(`thm:grassmannian_universal_property`, first ingredient): the chart images
2430	`{ι_I(U^I)}` cover the glued scheme, and on each member the restriction of
2431	`universalQuotient` is identified with `O^d` by transporting the descent restriction
2432	isomorphism `universalQuotient_restrictionIso` along the factorization
2433	`ι_I = isoOpensRange.hom ≫ opensRange.ι`. -/
2434	theorem universalQuotient_isLocallyFreeOfRank (d r : ℕ) :
2435	    SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank (universalQuotient d r) d := by
2436	  refine ⟨(theGlueData d r).J, fun I => ((theGlueData d r).ι I).opensRange, ?_, fun I => ?_⟩
2437	  · rw [eq_top_iff]
2438	    intro x _
2439	    obtain ⟨I, y, rfl⟩ := (theGlueData d r).ι_jointly_surjective x
2440	    exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨I, y, rfl⟩
2441	  · -- transport the chart restriction iso along `ι_I = isoOpensRange.hom ≫ opensRange.ι`,
2442	    -- inverting the chart-parametrization iso via the pullback pseudofunctor
2443	    refine ⟨?_⟩
2444	    letI ι := (theGlueData d r).ι I
2445	    letI e := ι.isoOpensRange
2446	    exact (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId _).symm.app _ ≪≫
2447	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (Iso.inv_hom_id e).symm).app _ ≪≫
2448	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp e.inv e.hom).app _).symm ≪≫
2449	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback e.inv).mapIso
2450	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp e.hom ι.opensRange.ι).app (universalQuotient d r) ≪≫
2451	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (ι.isoOpensRange_hom_ι)).app (universalQuotient d r) ≪≫
2452	          universalQuotient_restrictionIso d r I) ≪≫
2453	      Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso e.inv (Fin d) ≪≫
2454	      SheafOfModules.freeFunctor.mapIso (Equiv.ulift.symm.toIso)

2481	/-! ### The Nitsure §1 inverse construction: chart loci, chart matrices, chart morphisms
2482	
2483	For a rank-`d` quotient `x = ⟨F, q⟩` on `T` and a size-`d` subset `I ⊆ Fin r`, the
2484	*chart composite* is `s_I ≫ q : O_T^d ⟶ F` (the `I`-indexed coordinate inclusion
2485	followed by the quotient map) and the *chart locus* `T_I ⊆ T` is the largest open on
2486	which it restricts to an isomorphism. The loci are open by construction (a supremum of
2487	opens), cover `T` (Nakayama at each point), and over `T_I` the quotient is presented by
2488	a `d × r` matrix of sections whose `I`-minor is the identity — its complementary
2489	entries determine a ring map `R^I ⟶ Γ(T_I, O)` and hence a morphism `T_I ⟶ U^I` by the
2490	Γ–Spec adjunction. These glue to the inverse `grPointOfRankQuotient` of the universal
2491	property. -/
2492	
2493	/-- The **iso-locus** of a morphism of sheaves of modules on a scheme: the supremum
2494	(union) of all opens `U` such that the pullback of `φ` to the open subscheme `U` is an
2495	isomorphism. This is the largest open on which `φ` is invertible. Project-local
2496	(Mathlib has no iso-locus for morphisms of sheaves of modules). -/
2497	def isoLocus {X : Scheme.{0}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N) : X.Opens :=
2498	  sSup {U : X.Opens | IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).map φ)}
2499	
2500	/-- Membership in the iso-locus: `t ∈ isoLocus φ` iff some open neighbourhood of `t`
2501	pulls `φ` back to an isomorphism. Project-local. -/
2502	lemma mem_isoLocus {X : Scheme.{0}} {M N : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} {t : X} :
2503	    t ∈ isoLocus φ
2504	      ↔ ∃ U : X.Opens, IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).map φ) ∧ t ∈ U := by
2505	  simp only [isoLocus, TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_sSup, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
2506	
2507	/-- Restriction stability of pullback-invertibility: if `φ` pulls back to an
2508	isomorphism on `U` and `W ≤ U`, it pulls back to an isomorphism on `W` — transport
2509	along the pseudofunctor comparison `pullback W.ι ≅ pullback U.ι ⋙ pullback (homOfLE)`.
2510	Project-local. -/
2511	lemma isIso_pullback_map_of_le {X : Scheme.{0}} {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N)
2512	    {W U : X.Opens} (e : W ≤ U)
2513	    (hU : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).map φ)) :
2514	    IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback W.ι).map φ) := by
2515	  have h : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι ⋙
2516	      Scheme.Modules.pullback (X.homOfLE e)).map φ) := by
2517	    change IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (X.homOfLE e)).map
2518	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).map φ))
2519	    exact inferInstance
2520	  exact (NatIso.isIso_map_iff
2521	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp (X.homOfLE e) U.ι ≪≫
2522	      Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr (X.homOfLE_ι e)) φ).mp h

2524	/-- The **chart composite** `s_I ≫ q : O_T^d ⟶ F` (Nitsure §1): the `I`-indexed

2526	quotient `x`. Project-local. -/
2527	noncomputable def chartComposite {T : Scheme.{0}} {r d : ℕ} (x : RankQuotient r d T)
2528	    (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
2529	    SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) (Fin d) ⟶ x.F :=

2532	/-- The **chart locus** `T_I ⊆ T` of a rank-`d` quotient (Nitsure §1): the largest open

2536	  isoLocus (chartComposite x I hI)

2538	/-- **Right-invertible matrices over a field have an invertible column minor**: a
2539	`d × r` matrix with a right inverse has an invertible `d × d` submatrix on the columns
2540	of some size-`d` subset `I`, enumerated by `I.orderIsoOfFin` (the spelling used by
2541	`chartComposite`). Pure linear algebra: the columns span `K^d`, a spanning set contains
2542	a basis, and a square matrix with independent columns is invertible. Project-local
2543	helper for the Nakayama covering step. -/
2544	private lemma exists_isUnit_submatrix {K : Type} [Field K] {d r : ℕ}
2545	    (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) K) (G : Matrix (Fin r) (Fin d) K) (hMG : M * G = 1) :
2546	    ∃ (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d),
2547	      IsUnit (M.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))) := by
2548	  classical
2549	  -- the columns of `M` span `K^d` (a right inverse makes `mulVec` surjective)
2550	  have hspan : Submodule.span K (Set.range M.col) = ⊤ := by
2551	    rw [← Matrix.range_mulVecLin, LinearMap.range_eq_top]
2552	    intro v
2553	    refine ⟨G.mulVec v, ?_⟩
2554	    show M.mulVec (G.mulVec v) = v
2555	    rw [Matrix.mulVec_mulVec, hMG, Matrix.one_mulVec]
2556	  -- extract a linearly independent spanning subset `b` of the column set
2557	  obtain ⟨b, hbsub, hbspan, hbind⟩ := exists_linearIndependent K (Set.range M.col)
2558	  rw [hspan] at hbspan
2559	  haveI : Fintype b := ((Set.finite_range M.col).subset hbsub).fintype
2560	  -- `b` is a basis, so it has exactly `d` elements
2561	  let B : Module.Basis b K (Fin d → K) :=
2562	    Module.Basis.mk hbind (by rw [Subtype.range_coe, hbspan])
2563	  have hcard : Fintype.card b = d := by
2564	    have h1 := Module.finrank_eq_card_basis B
2565	    rw [Module.finrank_pi K, Fintype.card_fin] at h1
2566	    exact h1.symm
2567	  -- choose a column index for each element of `b`
2568	  have hchoice : ∀ v : b, ∃ j : Fin r, M.col j = (v : Fin d → K) := fun v => hbsub v.2
2569	  choose φ hφ using hchoice
2570	  have hφinj : Function.Injective φ := fun u v huv => by
2571	    apply Subtype.ext
2572	    rw [← hφ u, ← hφ v, huv]
2573	  have hIcard : (Finset.univ.image φ).card = d := by
2574	    rw [Finset.card_image_of_injective _ hφinj, Finset.card_univ, hcard]
2575	  refine ⟨Finset.univ.image φ, hIcard, ?_⟩
2576	  -- each enumerated column lies in `b`
2577	  have hcol : ∀ j : Fin d,
2578	      M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j : Fin r)) ∈ b := by
2579	    intro j
2580	    obtain ⟨v, -, hv⟩ := Finset.mem_image.mp
2581	      ((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j).2
2582	    rw [← hv, hφ v]
2583	    exact v.2
2584	  set c : Fin d → b :=
2585	    fun j => ⟨M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j : Fin r)), hcol j⟩
2586	    with hcdef
2587	  have hcinj : Function.Injective c := by
2588	    intro j j' hjj
2589	    have hcols : M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j : Fin r))
2590	        = M.col (((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j' : Fin r)) :=
2591	      congrArg Subtype.val hjj
2592	    obtain ⟨v, -, hv⟩ := Finset.mem_image.mp
2593	      ((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j).2
2594	    obtain ⟨v', -, hv'⟩ := Finset.mem_image.mp
2595	      ((Finset.univ.image φ).orderIsoOfFin hIcard j').2
2596	    have hvv' : v = v' := by
2597	      apply Subtype.ext
2598	      calc (v : Fin d → K)
2599	          = M.col (φ v) := (hφ v).symm

1824	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\) and \(\langle \mathcal{F}', q' \rangle\) are
1825	  \emph{equivalent} if there is an isomorphism \(f : \mathcal{F}
1826	  \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{F}'\) with \(f \circ q = q'\); equivalently
1827	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\) as subsheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\). Set
1828	  \[
1829	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)
1830	      \;=\; \bigl\{\, \text{equivalence classes } \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle
1831	        \text{ of rank-}d\text{ quotients of } \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \,\bigr\}.
1832	  \]
1833	  For a morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\), the map
1834	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(\varphi)\) sends \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\)
1835	  to the pulled-back quotient \(\langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{F},\, \varphi^{*}q
1836	  \rangle\); this is well-defined on equivalence classes because pullback is right
1837	  exact and preserves local freeness of rank \(d\). (As \(\mathcal{F}\) is locally
1838	  free of rank \(d\), surjectivity is preserved and the support is automatically
1839	  proper -- all of \(T'\) -- so this is the special case
1840	  \(E = \mathcal{O}^{r}\), \(X = S = \operatorname{base}\) of the general Quot
1841	  functor of \cref{def:quot_functor}.)
1842	\end{definition}
1843	
1844	\section{The universal property}
1845	\label{sec:grquot_universal}
1846	
1847	The representability theorem \cref{thm:grassmannian_universal_property} has two halves:
1848	the forward map \(\varphi \mapsto \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u\rangle\)
1849	and its inverse, which manufactures a morphism \(T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) out of a rank-\(d\)
1850	quotient \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) on \(T\). The forward map needs the tautological
1851	quotient to actually be a quotient -- that \(u\) is an epimorphism
1852	(\cref{lem:tautologicalQuotient_epi}). The inverse is built Zariski-locally and glued: for
1853	each size-\(d\) subset \(I\) one isolates the open locus \(T_I \subseteq T\) where the
1854	\(I\)-indexed columns of \(q\) form a basis of \(\mathcal{F}\), classifies \(T_I \to U^I\)
1855	there, and glues over the cover \(\{T_I\}\). The blocks of this section supply the four
1856	ingredients of that inverse: the iso-locus of a sheaf morphism (\cref{def:isoLocus}) and
1857	the fact that a morphism becomes invertible after restriction to its iso-locus
1858	(\cref{lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}); the chart locus \(T_I\) (\cref{def:chartLocus})
1859	and the proof that the chart loci cover \(T\) (\cref{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover}); and the
1860	glued classifying morphism itself (\cref{def:grPointOfRankQuotient}).
1861	
1862	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1863	[The tautological quotient is an epimorphism]
1864	  \label{lem:tautologicalQuotient_epi}
1865	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.tautologicalQuotient_epi}
1866	  \uses{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi, def:tautological_quotient}
1867	  The tautological quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r}
1868	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}) is an epimorphism of
1869	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}\)-modules.
1870	\end{lemma}
1871	\begin{proof}
1872	  \uses{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi, def:tautological_quotient}
1873	  Being an epimorphism of sheaves of modules is local on the base: a morphism is epi
1874	  precisely when its restriction to every member of an open cover is epi, since the
1875	  cokernel is computed chartwise and a sheaf vanishing on a cover vanishes. The charts
1876	  \(\{U^I\}\) cover \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), and over \(U^I\) the tautological quotient,
1877	  transported through the trivialisation \(\mathcal{U}|_{U^I} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
1878	  of \cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}, is the chart quotient \(u^I :
1879	  \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\) (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}).
1880	  Each \(u^I\) is a split epimorphism: its \(I\)-minor is the identity \(1_{d\times d}\),
1881	  so the coordinate inclusion \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \hookrightarrow
1882	  \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) on the \(I\)-columns is a section of \(u^I\), whence \(u^I\) is
1883	  epi (\cref{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi}). As \(u^I\) is epi on every chart, \(u\) is epi.
1884	\end{proof}
1885	
1886	\begin{definition}\leanok
1887	[Iso-locus of a morphism of sheaves of modules]
1888	  \label{def:isoLocus}
1889	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isoLocus}
1890	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(\varphi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) a morphism of sheaves
1891	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. The \emph{iso-locus} \(\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi) \subseteq X\)
1892	  is the open subscheme
1893	  \[
1894	    \mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)
1895	      \;=\; \bigsqcup\,\bigl\{\, U \subseteq X \text{ open} \;:\;
1896	        \varphi|_U := (\iota_U^{*}\varphi) \text{ is an isomorphism} \,\bigr\},
1897	  \]
1898	  the supremum (union) of all opens \(U\) over which the pullback \(\iota_U^{*}\varphi\) of
1899	  \(\varphi\) to the open subscheme \(U\) is an isomorphism of sheaves of
1900	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules. Equivalently, a point \(t \in X\) lies in
1901	  \(\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)\) iff \(\varphi\) restricts to an isomorphism over some open
1902	  neighbourhood of \(t\). It is the largest open on which \(\varphi\) is invertible; Mathlib
1903	  has no iso-locus primitive for morphisms of sheaves of modules, so it is set up directly
1904	  as this supremum.
1905	\end{definition}
1906	
1907	\begin{lemma}[Stalk-wise iso criterion]
1908	  \label{lem:isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso_mathlib}
1909	  \lean{TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso}
1910	  \mathlibok
1911	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1912	  Let \(\mathcal{F}, \mathcal{G}\) be sheaves on a topological space \(X\) valued in a
1913	  concrete category whose forgetful functor reflects isomorphisms and preserves limits and
1914	  filtered colimits, and let \(f : \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{G}\) be a morphism. If the
1915	  induced map on stalks \(f_t : \mathcal{F}_t \to \mathcal{G}_t\) is an isomorphism for every
1916	  point \(t \in X\), then \(f\) is an isomorphism.
1917	\end{lemma}
1918	
1919	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1920	[A morphism is invertible over its iso-locus]
1921	  \label{lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}
1922	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}
1923	  \uses{def:isoLocus, lem:isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso_mathlib}
1924	  For a morphism \(\varphi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) of sheaves of
1925	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, the pullback of \(\varphi\) to the iso-locus
1926	  (\cref{def:isoLocus}),
1927	  \[
1928	    (\iota_{\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)})^{*}\varphi
1929	      \;:\;
1930	    (\iota_{\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)})^{*}\mathcal{M}
1931	      \;\longrightarrow\;
1932	    (\iota_{\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)})^{*}\mathcal{N},
1933	  \]
1934	  is an isomorphism.
1935	\end{lemma}
1936	\begin{proof}
1937	  \uses{def:isoLocus, lem:isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso_mathlib}
1938	  Being an isomorphism is a stalk-local property: by the stalk-wise criterion
1939	  (\cref{lem:isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso_mathlib}) it suffices to show that the pulled-back
1940	  morphism induces an isomorphism on the stalk at every point \(t \in
1941	  \mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)\). By the definition of the iso-locus as a union of opens on
1942	  which \(\varphi\) restricts to an isomorphism (\cref{def:isoLocus}), each such \(t\) has an
1943	  open neighbourhood \(U \subseteq \mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)\) with \(\iota_U^{*}\varphi\) an
1944	  isomorphism; restriction of an isomorphism is an isomorphism, and pullback commutes with
1945	  taking stalks, so the stalk of the morphism at \(t\) coincides with the stalk of
1946	  \(\iota_U^{*}\varphi\) at \(t\), which is invertible. Hence every stalk map is an
1947	  isomorphism, and the criterion concludes that the global pullback over
1948	  \(\mathrm{isoLocus}(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism.
1949	\end{proof}

1951	\begin{definition}\leanok
1952	[Chart locus of a rank-\(d\) quotient]
1953	  \label{def:chartLocus}
1954	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartLocus}
1955	  \uses{def:isoLocus, def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_rankQuotient}
1956	  Let \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) be a rank-\(d\) quotient of
1957	  \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\) on a scheme \(T\) (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}) and \(I \subseteq
1958	  \{1,\dots,r\}\) a size-\(d\) subset. The \emph{chart composite} is the morphism of sheaves
1959	  of modules
1960	  \[
1961	    s_I \circ q
1962	      \;:\;
1963	    \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}
1964	      \overset{\ s_I\ }{\hookrightarrow} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
1965	      \xrightarrow{\ q\ } \mathcal{F},
1966	  \]
1967	  obtained by precomposing the quotient \(q\) with the inclusion \(s_I\) of the
1968	  \(I\)-indexed coordinates of the trivial bundle. The \emph{chart locus}
1969	  \(T_I = \mathrm{chartLocus}(x, I) \subseteq T\) is the iso-locus
1970	  (\cref{def:isoLocus}) of this chart composite:
1971	  \[
1972	    T_I \;=\; \mathrm{isoLocus}(s_I \circ q),
1973	  \]
1974	  the largest open of \(T\) over which the \(I\)-columns of \(q\) form a basis of
1975	  \(\mathcal{F}\) -- i.e.\ over which \(s_I \circ q\) is an isomorphism. Over \(T_I\) the
1976	  quotient \(q\) is therefore presented, after trivialising \(\mathcal{F}\) by
1977	  \((s_I \circ q)^{-1}\), by a \(d \times r\) matrix whose \(I\)-minor is the identity,
1978	  exactly the shape of the universal matrix \(X^I\) realising the chart quotient \(u^I\)
1979	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}).
1980	\end{definition}
1981	
1982	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1983	[The chart loci cover \(T\)]
1984	  \label{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover}
1985	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartLocus_isOpenCover}
1986	  \uses{def:chartLocus, def:gr_rankQuotient, lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}
1987	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
1988	  % sub-section "Construction by gluing together affine patches" (read from
1989	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L823-L828).
1990	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1991	  % "For any $J\subset \{1,\ldots, r\}$ with $\#(J) =d$, let
1992	  %  $P^I_J = \det(X^I_J) \in \Z[X^I]$
1993	  %  where $X^I_J$ is the $J$ th minor of $X^I$.
1994	  %  Let $U^I_J = \spec \Z[X^I, 1/P^I_J]$
1995	  %  the open subscheme of $U^I$ where $P^I_J$ is invertible. This means
1996	  %  the $d\times d$-matrix $X^I_J$ admits an inverse $(X^I_J)^{-1}$
1997	  %  on $U^I_J$."
1998	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
1999	  For a rank-\(d\) quotient \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\)
2000	  on \(T\) (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}), the family of chart loci
2001	  \(\{\,T_I = \mathrm{chartLocus}(x, I)\,\}_{\#(I) = d}\) (\cref{def:chartLocus}) is an open
2002	  cover of \(T\): \(\bigcup_{\#(I)=d} T_I = T\).
2003	\end{lemma}
2004	\begin{proof}
2005	  \uses{def:chartLocus, def:gr_rankQuotient}
2006	  Each \(T_I\) is open, being an iso-locus (\cref{def:isoLocus}). It remains to show the
2007	  \(T_I\) cover \(T\), i.e.\ that every point \(t \in T\) lies in some \(T_I\). Fix \(t\) and
2008	  work in an open neighbourhood \(V \ni t\) trivialising \(\mathcal{F}\),
2009	  \(\mathcal{F}|_V \cong \mathcal{O}_V^{\,d}\) (such \(V\) exists because \(\mathcal{F}\) is
2010	  locally free of rank \(d\)); over \(V\) the quotient \(q\) is presented by a \(d \times r\)
2011	  matrix \(M\) of sections of \(\mathcal{O}_V\). Because \(q\) is an epimorphism
2012	  (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}), its reduction modulo the maximal ideal of the local ring at
2013	  \(t\) -- the fibre map \(q(t) : \kappa(t)^{r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\otimes\kappa(t)
2014	  \cong \kappa(t)^{d}\) -- is surjective. A surjection of vector spaces \(\kappa(t)^{r}
2015	  \twoheadrightarrow \kappa(t)^{d}\) carries some \(d\) of the \(r\) standard basis vectors to
2016	  a basis of \(\kappa(t)^{d}\); choosing such an index set \(I\) of size \(d\), the
2017	  corresponding \(d \times d\) minor \(M_I(t)\) is invertible over \(\kappa(t)\), i.e.\ its
2018	  determinant \(P_I = \det(M_I)\) does not vanish at \(t\). On the basic open
2019	  \(V_{P_I} = \{P_I \ne 0\} \subseteq V\), the section \(P_I\) is a unit, so the matrix
2020	  \(M_I\) is invertible and the chart composite \(s_I \circ q\) restricts to an isomorphism
2021	  there (this is the source mechanism: the locus where a minor determinant is invertible is
2022	  open, and the minor matrix admits an inverse on it). The implication ``minor invertible at
2023	  \(t\) \(\Rightarrow\) chart composite invertible on a neighbourhood'' is the Nakayama step:
2024	  invertibility of \((s_I \circ q)(t)\) on the fibre, together with finite generation, forces
2025	  invertibility on a neighbourhood. Hence \(t \in V_{P_I} \subseteq T_I\), and the \(T_I\)
2026	  cover \(T\).
2027	\end{proof}
2028	
2029	\begin{definition}\leanok
2030	[The morphism induced by a rank-\(d\) quotient]
2031	  \label{def:grPointOfRankQuotient}
2032	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.grPointOfRankQuotient}
2033	  \uses{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover, lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map,
2034	    def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle,
2035	    def:gr_glued_scheme}
2036	  Let \(x = \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) be a rank-\(d\) quotient of
2037	  \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}\) on a scheme \(T\) (\cref{def:gr_rankQuotient}). The morphism
2038	  \[
2039	    \mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(x) \;:\; T \;\longrightarrow\; \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)
2040	  \]
2041	  is constructed as follows. Over each chart locus \(T_I = \mathrm{chartLocus}(x, I)\)
2042	  (\cref{def:chartLocus}) the chart composite \(s_I \circ q\) is an isomorphism
2043	  (\cref{lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}), so trivialising \(\mathcal{F}|_{T_I}\) by its
2044	  inverse presents \(q|_{T_I}\) as a \(d \times r\) matrix \(M^I\) of global functions on
2045	  \(T_I\) whose \(I\)-minor is the identity. Its \(d(r-d)\) complementary entries determine a
2046	  unique \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to \Gamma(T_I,
2047	  \mathcal{O}_{T_I})\), \(x^I_{p,q} \mapsto M^I_{p,q}\), hence by the \(\Gamma\)--\(\Spec\)
2048	  adjunction a unique \emph{chart morphism} \(\varphi_I : T_I \to U^I\)
2049	  (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) with \(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\), and thus
2050	  \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I = q|_{T_I}\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}). The chart loci cover \(T\)
2051	  (\cref{lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover}), and on each overlap \(T_I \cap T_J\) the two
2052	  presenting matrices are change-of-basis related by the invertible \(I\)-columns minor,
2053	  \(M^J = (M^I_J)^{-1} M^I = \varphi_I^{*}\theta_{I,J}(X^J)\)
2054	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}); hence the composites \(T_I \xrightarrow{\varphi_I} U^I
2055	  \hookrightarrow \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) with the chart immersions agree on overlaps after the
2056	  transition \(\theta_{I,J}\). By the cocycle condition (\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}) these
2057	  chart-local morphisms glue to a single morphism \(T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\)
2058	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}); this glued morphism is
2059	  \(\mathrm{grPointOfRankQuotient}(x)\), the local-to-global inverse of the universal
2060	  property: pulling the tautological quotient back along it recovers \(\langle \mathcal{F},
2061	  q\rangle\) up to equivalence.
2062	\end{definition}

2064	\begin{theorem}
2065	
2066	\leanok
2067	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) represents the Grassmannian functor]
2068	  \label{thm:grassmannian_universal_property}
2069	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.represents}
2070	  \uses{def:grassmannian_functor, def:tautological_quotient,
2071	    def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_affine_chart,
2072	    def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle}
2073	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
2074	  % Exercise (2) "Grassmannian as a Quot scheme" (read from
2075	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L566).
2076	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2077	  % "Show that $\grass(r,d)$ together with the quotient
2078	  %  $u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$ represents the
2079	  %  contravariant functor
2080	  %  $$\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$$
2081	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any $T$ the
2082	  %  set of all equivalence classes
2083	  %  $\langle \F, q\rangle$ of quotients $q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F$
2084	  %  where $\F$ is a locally free sheaf on $T$ of rank $d$.
2085	  %  Therefore, $\quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}$
2086	  %  exists, and equals $\grass(r,d)$."
2087	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2).}
2088	  The Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}),
2089	  together with the tautological quotient \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\)
2090	  (\cref{def:tautological_quotient}), represents the functor
2091	  \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:grassmannian_functor}). That is, for
2092	  every scheme \(T\) the natural map
2093	  \[
2094	    \operatorname{Hom}(T, \mathrm{Gr}(r,d))
2095	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
2096	    \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T),
2097	    \qquad
2098	    \bigl(\,\varphi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\,\bigr)
2099	      \;\longmapsto\;
2100	    \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U},\, \varphi^{*}u \rangle,
2101	  \]
2102	  is a bijection, natural in \(T\). Consequently the Quot scheme
2103	  \(\quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/
2104	  \mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) of rank-\(d\) quotients of the trivial rank-\(r\) sheaf
2105	  exists and equals \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\); the tautological quotient is the
2106	  universal family.
2107	\end{theorem}
2108	
2109	\begin{proof}
2110	  \uses{def:tautological_quotient, def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf,
2111	    def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix,
2112	    def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle, def:gr_glued_scheme,
2113	    def:grassmannian_functor, lem:tautologicalQuotient_epi,
2114	    def:grPointOfRankQuotient, lem:chartLocus_isOpenCover, def:chartLocus,
2115	    lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map}
2116	  Naturality of \(\varphi \mapsto \langle \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u
2117	  \rangle\) is immediate: pullback is functorial and \(\varphi^{*}u\) is again a
2118	  surjection onto a rank-\(d\) locally free sheaf, so the assignment defines a
2119	  morphism of functors \(\operatorname{Hom}(-,\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)) \to
2120	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\). It remains to show it is a bijection for each
2121	  \(T\); we exhibit an inverse, constructed Zariski-locally on \(T\) and glued.
2122	
2123	  \emph{From a quotient to a morphism.} Let \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle \in
2124	  \operatorname{Grass}(r,d)(T)\), so \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow
2125	  \mathcal{F}\) with \(\mathcal{F}\) locally free of rank \(d\). For a size-\(d\)
2126	  subset \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) let \(T_I \subseteq T\) be the locus where
2127	  the composite
2128	  \[
2129	    \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}
2130	      \overset{\ \iota_I\ }{\hookrightarrow} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,r}
2131	      \xrightarrow{\ q\ } \mathcal{F},
2132	  \]
2133	  with \(\iota_I\) the inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates, is an
2134	  isomorphism. This locus is open: in a local trivialisation of \(\mathcal{F}\)
2135	  the composite is a \(d \times d\) matrix and \(T_I\) is the non-vanishing locus
2136	  of its determinant. The \(T_I\) cover \(T\): at any point \(t\), the
2137	  \(d\)-dimensional fibre quotient \(q(t) : \kappa(t)^{r} \twoheadrightarrow
2138	  \mathcal{F}\otimes\kappa(t)\) is surjective, so some \(d\) of the \(r\) standard
2139	  basis vectors map to a basis of the \(d\)-dimensional fibre, i.e.\ \(t \in T_I\)
2140	  for that \(I\) (by Nakayama the isomorphism then holds in a neighbourhood).
2141	
2142	  On \(T_I\), trivialise \(\mathcal{F}\) using \(\iota_I^{*}\): the quotient
2143	  becomes a surjection \(q_I : \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{T_I}^{\,d}\)
2144	  represented by a \(d \times r\) matrix \(M^I\) whose \(I\)-th minor \(M^I_I\) is
2145	  the identity \(1_{d \times d}\) -- exactly the shape of the universal matrix
2146	  \(X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}). The \(d(r-d)\) free entries of \(M^I\)
2147	  are global functions on \(T_I\) and hence determine a unique
2148	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to
2149	  \Gamma(T_I, \mathcal{O}_{T_I})\) sending \(x^I_{p,q}\) to the corresponding entry
2150	  of \(M^I\), i.e.\ a unique morphism \(\varphi_I : T_I \to U^I\)
2151	  (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) with \(\varphi_I^{*}X^I = M^I\) and thus
2152	  \(\varphi_I^{*}u^I = q_I\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}).
2153	
2154	  \emph{Gluing.} On the overlap \(T_I \cap T_J\) the two trivialisations differ by
2155	  the change-of-basis \((M^I_J)^{-1}\), which is exactly the bundle transition
2156	  \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) of \(\mathcal{U}\)
2157	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}); concretely
2158	  \(M^J = (M^I_J)^{-1} M^I = \varphi_I^{*}\theta_{I,J}(X^J)\)

2164	  construction \(\varphi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle = \langle \mathcal{F},
2165	  q \rangle\), since this holds on each \(T_I\) and the equivalence class is
2166	  determined by \(\ker(q)\).

2168	  \emph{Uniqueness.} If \(\psi : T \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) also satisfies
2169	  \(\psi^{*}\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle = \langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\), then
2170	  on \(\psi^{-1}(U^I)\) the pulled-back tautological quotient \(\psi^{*}u^I\)
2171	  equals \(q\) in the \(I\)-trivialisation, which forces \(\psi^{*}X^I = M^I\) and
2172	  hence \(\psi = \varphi_I\) there; as the \(U^I\) cover \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\),
2173	  \(\psi = \varphi\). Thus \(\varphi\) is the unique morphism inducing
2174	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q \rangle\), and \(\varphi \mapsto \langle
2175	  \varphi^{*}\mathcal{U}, \varphi^{*}u \rangle\) is bijective.
2176	
2177	  Therefore \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) with \(\langle \mathcal{U}, u \rangle\) is a fine
2178	  moduli space: it represents \(\operatorname{Grass}(r,d)\), so
2179	  \(\quot^{\,d,\,\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}}_{\oplus^{r}\mathcal{O}_{\mathbb{Z}}/
2180	  \mathbb{Z}/\mathbb{Z}}\) exists and equals \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\).
2181	\end{proof}

3235	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h

3238	        ↔ IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).map (chartComposite y I hI)) := by
3239	    intro U

3241	      inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).mapIso f).hom)

3245	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).map f.hom
3246	        = (Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).map (chartComposite y I hI) :=
3247	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).map_comp _ _).symm.trans
3248	        (congrArg ((Scheme.Modules.pullback U.ι).map) (chartComposite_rel f hf I hI))

3250	    exact (isIso_comp_right_iff _ _).symm
